title: CP never sets StartSpawn.Generation -> reconcile orphan arm kills every fresh pod
description: Since sp-537t.2 (1d76622, reconcileInventory adopt+orphan arms): Scheduler.Provision sends StartSpawn WITHOUT Generation (proto field 5 exists, never set; Provision does not even take a gen param). Node labels/reports the pod as gen 0; CP live container row is gen>=1 (Create inserts gen 1, ClaimStarting gen max+1). adoptOrStop: c.Generation(>=1) != 0 -> orphan arm -> StopSpawn(id, 0); node staleGen never fences gen 0 -> stopSpawn kills the pod, usually mid-ACP-handshake -> 'agent not ready: pump stopped' -> provision failed. Surviving spawns are killed on the next 5s heartbeat; CP then logs 'active spawn(s) not reported -> unreachable'. Fix: thread gen through Provision -> StartSpawn (CreateSpawn gen 1, Resume/Recreate ClaimStarting gen). Also: e2e suite gap — no test covers heartbeat reconcile during/after spawn start.
close_reason: Fixed in 6eab9df: Provision threads the live row generation into StartSpawn (create=1, resume/recreate=ClaimStarting gen). Regression tests at scheduler, CP-reconcile, and e2e (heartbeat-survival + second echo) levels; e2e verified red against reintroduced bug.

title: AS-signed session authority: sessions signed by AS, node verifies offline; self-hosted + cloud split model
description: Move session-token signing from CP to AS (offline-verified by nodes via pinned AS pubkey) so a compromised CP can't forge session authority. Self-hosted: AS-signed proof of account O, node checks O==accountId. Cloud: AS attests identity / CP supplies routing / node enforces ownership+tenancy. See sp-ova §7a.
close_reason: AS session-token MECHANISM done (commit 6b34091): internal/sessiontoken Ed25519 Sign/Verify (offline; rejects tamper/wrong-key/expiry) + authsvc session key/IssueSessionToken/SessionPubKey + /session/pubkey. Live-flow consumption (CP issue on session-open, node verify + self-hosted/cloud split on attach) = follow-up sp-rjr (spans WS + node relay + web, overlaps E4). TDD green; build/vet/fmt clean.

title: Tenancy gate: self-hosted single-tenant (owner==node accountId), cloud multi-tenant
description: CP placement: a spawn owned by O may go to any cloud node OR a self-hosted node whose verified SAN accountId==O; NEVER place O's spawn on a self-hosted node bound to O'!=O. Node also refuses to run a spawn whose owner != its accountId. Review status is NOT a trust boundary (apps run anywhere). See sp-ova §6.
close_reason: Tenancy gate done (commit ec77df3): registry.PickFor enforces cloud=multi-tenant / self-hosted=owner-only; placementFor returns {Owner} for all tiers (review tier only gates unverified-author authorization). Fixes latent bug (reviewed app could land on another owner's self-hosted node). Node-side defense-in-depth refusal -> sp-1g3 (needs node cert identity). TDD TestPickForTenancy; full repo 28 green.

title: CP node-auth enforcement + insecure/enforced modes + drop self-asserted Register fields
description: Enforced mode: mTLS listener for NodeService pinned to the Root CA; derive {nodeId,accountId,class} from the verified peer-cert SAN in runNode (NEVER from Register). Insecure mode: current plaintext h2c on a dev port for unit/e2e/local. Remove self-asserted node_class/node_owner from proto/node + runNode. CP holds only public pins. See sp-ova §5/§8/§10.
notes: PROGRESS (7826958): nodeauth core landed (internal/cp/nodeauth) — DeriveIdentity (cert->identity, rejects no-cert + forged-cloud), WithIdentity/IdentityFromContext, Middleware(insecure|enforced). TDD green. REMAINING: (1) runNode prefers ctx identity over self-asserted Register (insecure keeps Register fallback so existing tests pass); (2) cmd/cp main.go enforced-mode mTLS listener (ClientAuth: RequireAnyClientCert + middleware verify against pinned root) wrapping the NodeService handler, insecure=current h2c; (3) node-side presents cert (-> sp-1g3).
close_reason: CP enforcement done (commit fd2a12a): nodeauth core + runNode takes id from verified cert (ignores self-asserted Register when authenticated; insecure keeps fallback) + cmd/cp NODE_AUTH_MODE insecure(h2c, default)/enforced(dedicated mTLS listener, RequireAnyClientCert + middleware verify vs pinned root). Full proto-field REMOVAL deferred until enforced-only (would break node sender + insecure tests); fields are ignored-when-authenticated for now. TDD green (TestRegisterPrefersVerifiedIdentity); full repo suite 28 pkgs green.

title: Node enrollment via AS: owner-authenticated one-time token, node keypair+CSR, AS issues self-hosted cert
description: AS authenticates the requesting owner (it is the IdP) and issues a one-time, short-lived, single-use, account-scoped enrollment token bound to that accountId. Node generates its own keypair (private key never leaves the box), submits CSR+token; AS verifies and issues a self-hosted cert with SAN <nodeId>.<accountId>.self-hosted.nodes.spawnery.internal from the held intermediate. See sp-ova §3/§4.
close_reason: Enrollment vertical done (commit 563dd42): pki NewNodeCSR/SignCSR (proof-of-possession; CA-chosen SAN) + CSR PEM; authsvc IssueEnrollmentToken (one-time, account-bound, ~10m, atomic consume), Enroll, POST /enroll, RunEnroll node client. Cert bound to token's account + node's own key, class always self-hosted. Owner OAuth gating = E4 (out of scope; token endpoint not exposed unauthenticated). TDD green; build/vet/gofmt clean.

title: Auth Service skeleton: own container, holds self-hosted intermediate key, publishes verification pubkeys
description: New AS service (separate container/node) = identity root of trust. Holds the self-hosted intermediate private key (never exported) + the AS session-signing key. Health/config; insecure vs enforced mode; publishes its verification pubkeys (Root CA pin distribution, AS session pubkey). No issuance logic yet. See sp-ova §1/§10.
close_reason: AS skeleton done (worktree branch, commit 972fac2): internal/authsvc Service holds the self-hosted intermediate + issues self-hosted-only certs (verify against published root), Load from PEM, HTTP Handler (/healthz + /ca/root export), cmd/authsvc container entrypoint w/ graceful shutdown. Enrollment-token auth = sp-0qc, AS-signed sessions = sp-3ca build on top. TDD green; build/vet/gofmt clean.

title: PKI/CA tooling + fixtures: Root CA, name-constrained cloud+self-hosted intermediates, per-env root, cloud leaf mint, hardcoded test Root CA
description: Foundation. Offline ceremony tooling to generate a Root CA and two intermediates: cloud (offline/operator) and self-hosted (name-constrained to self-hosted.nodes.spawnery.internal, later held by the AS). Cloud-leaf minting tool. Per-environment Root CA (no shared CA). A hardcoded TEST Root CA + cert fixtures for enforced-mode tests. Go crypto/x509 name-constraint create+verify; class encoded in DNS SAN. See sp-ova design §4/§8.
close_reason: pki library complete + tested (commit b941061 on worktree branch worktree-node-auth-sp-ova): Root CA, name-constrained cloud/self-hosted intermediates (self-hosted cryptographically can't forge cloud — proven load-bearing), IssueNode w/ SAN identity, Verify (chain+constraints+expiry)->Identity, PEM persistence, TLSCertificate. Offline-mint CLI + checked-in test-Root-CA fixture deferred to consumers (T9 e2e builds its own test CA via the API; a thin cmd/spawnery-ca wrapper can be added if/when ops needs it).

title: Implement node auth + Auth Service (sp-ova): unified identity, PKI, tenancy, AS sessions
description: Implementation of the sp-ova design (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-05-node-auth-unified-identity-design.md). Separate Auth Service as identity root of trust; CP holds only public pins; CA-cert node identity (SAN-encoded class via name-constrained intermediates, cloud intermediate offline); cloud multi-tenant / self-hosted single-tenant tenancy gate; AS-signed offline-verified sessions; insecure vs enforced modes with PKI-soundness e2e tests; per-env Root CA. Children are dependency-sequenced. Confidentiality (E2E relay sp-4n9/sp-zdd) and web-SPA-delivery (sp-ore) are tracked separately.
close_reason: All 9 children closed. Node-auth + Auth Service implemented end-to-end TDD on branch worktree-node-auth-sp-ova (9 commits b941061..3f56186): pki name-constrained CA, authsvc (enrollment + AS sessions), CP cert-derived identity + insecure/enforced modes + mTLS listener, tenancy gate, node mTLS dial, sessiontoken, clientverify, PKI-soundness e2e. Full repo 31 pkgs green; golangci-lint 0 issues; insecure default keeps dev/tests unchanged. Follow-ups filed: sp-rjr (live session-flow wiring), and consume-side client verify depends on sp-zdd/sp-gtm cert-vending + sp-ore web delivery.

title: [SECURITY/ARCH] gVisor breaks the shared-netns pod: agent can't reach sidecar on 127.0.0.1 under runsc
description:
LOCAL-VERIFIED (this host, runsc release-20260525.0). A real goose+sidecar spawn was driven under both runtimes via standalone spawnlet + spawnctl 'what is the secret word?':
- runc: goose reached the sidecar (127.0.0.1:8080), full ACP round-trip, real LLM reply ('The secret word is QUOKKA-4417'). WORKS.
- runsc: goose BOOTED + ACP-initialized + accepted the prompt (so goose IS gVisor-compatible — no syscall/ENOSYS issues), but failed with 'Network error: Could not connect to 127.0.0.1:8080'. The SIDECAR IS UNREACHABLE.
ROOT CAUSE: our pod is two Docker containers sharing a netns (agent started with --network=container:<sidecar>, reaches the sidecar's inference proxy on loopback). Under per-container gVisor, EACH container is its own Sentry with its OWN user-space netstack — Docker's container:<id> netns-sharing does NOT bridge the two Sentries' loopbacks, so the agent's 127.0.0.1 is not the sidecar's. The gVisor research flagged this: k8s puts a WHOLE POD in ONE runsc sandbox; Docker 'docker run' per-container does not.
=> gVisor adoption (sp-7k8) CANNOT use the current Docker two-container shared-netns pod. Options to design: (a) run the pod as ONE runsc sandbox (containerd CRI pod / k8s RuntimeClass — whole pod = one Sentry, shared loopback, the documented model); (b) collapse the sidecar into the agent container (single container = agent + inference-proxy process, one sandbox, loopback works); (c) separate netns + agent reaches sidecar via bridge IP:8080 (weakens isolation, cross-container exposure, and conflicts with the RFC1918 egress drop — messy). DESIGN-FIRST. This is a HARD prerequisite for Phase-2 gVisor on cloud; verified that goose, hardening (cap-drop), preflight, and the host egress floor all work under runsc — only the inter-container loopback breaks.
notes:
DECISION (user 2026-06-01): for RUNSC use a one-sandbox pod via containerd CRI (RunPodSandbox handler=runsc + 2 containers: sidecar+agent, separate mount/pid ns sharing the pod netns -> loopback works, KEY STAYS ISOLATED in the sidecar). For RUNC keep the current Docker two-container shared-netns approach unchanged. FEASIBILITY CONFIRMED on this host: containerd 1.7.24 CRI plugin enabled+ok, /run/containerd/containerd.sock live, containerd-shim-runsc-v1 present. STILL NEEDED: (1) containerd config — add a [plugins...cri.containerd.runtimes.runsc] handler (runtime_type io.containerd.runsc.v1) + restart; (2) CNI for pod-sandbox networking (cri plugin needs a CNI bridge plugin/config). EPIC SCOPE (large): node gains a CRI pod backend (k8s.io/cri-api gRPC client to the containerd sock) selected when CONTAINER_RUNTIME=runsc; manager.Create branches runc(Docker two-container) vs runsc(CRI pod); sub-systems: CRI RunPodSandbox/CreateContainer/StartContainer x2, CRI ImageService pull (containerd's k8s.io image namespace is SEPARATE from Docker's — images must be pulled there), stdio Attach to the agent container over CRI streaming for the ACP bridge, egress floor on the POD IP but the CNI bridge is not docker0 so DOCKER-USER may not be in-path -> floor likely needs a raw-FORWARD/custom host chain for CRI pods, RemovePodSandbox teardown, runsc preflight under CRI. Cleanest seam: a runtime.Pod abstraction both backends implement (Docker=2 containers shared netns; CRI=pod). DESIGN-FIRST.
RESOLVED + REAL-HOST VERIFIED (runsc release-20260525.0, this host). The one-sandbox CRI pod (handler=runsc, sidecar+agent sharing the pod netns) fixes agent<->sidecar on 127.0.0.1:8080 — the original sp-vaw break. BUT the runsc round-trip needed two further fixes found running E2E_TEST_RUNSC.md: (1) node<->agent ACP transport — gVisor isolates the in-sandbox abstract-UDS namespace from the host, so the runc-lane setns+UDS attach can't reach the adapter; switched the CRI lane to TCP on the pod IP (adapter listens tcp://0.0.0.0:7000, node dials podIP:7000). (2) POD_DNS — kubelet-less CRI pods inherited the 127.0.0.53 stub resolver so the sidecar couldn't resolve the model upstream. With these: full goose round-trip 'QUOKKA-4417', 5/5 create->teardown cycles with zero leaks, per-pod SPAWNLET-EGRESS floor enforced (metadata+RFC1918 dropped in-agent, public+DNS ok). Fixes committed (b4e1b4b). docker-29 ContainerIP compat also fixed in the same commit.

title: [SECURITY] CP must authenticate nodes + verify cloud-class — today NODE_CLASS/NODE_OWNER are self-asserted on an UNAUTHENTICATED stream (cloud-spoofing)
description:
CRITICAL TRUST GAP. The node<->CP Attach stream has NO auth (cmd/cp/main.go:103 'node side: no auth'); a node self-reports node_class + node_owner in its Register message (sp-2as/sp-t5p). So ANY node that can reach the CP can claim NODE_CLASS=cloud (and any node_owner) and be treated as a trusted, Spawnery-operated cloud node. Combined with class/owner-based routing (sp-t5p) and any future cross-user/secret data flow (vended storage tokens, other users' spawns, audit data), a malicious self-hosted node could SPOOF a cloud spawnlet and receive other users' secret data. The cloud/self-hosted trust split (sp-rpa floor enforcement, sp-t5p routing) currently RESTS ON unauthenticated self-assertion — only safe today because the demo runs solely Spawnery-operated nodes on a closed CP.

NOTE: there is NO node keypair / secret-passing crypto in the codebase yet (only crypto/tls transport + crypto/rand for ids). The 'spawnlets generate pubkeys for secret passing' is E0/E4 DESIGN, deferred in the demo cut — not built. So this must be designed, not just wired.

PROPOSED (user 2026-06-01): cloud spawnlets present a node identity cert whose pubkey is signed by Spawnery's ROOT CA; CP PINS that root and verifies the chain on Attach before honoring class=cloud or routing any cross-user/secret data. Self-hosted nodes are unauthenticated/lower-trust and may only run their OWN owner's spawns (aligns with sp-t5p: unverified->author's-own-node; a self-hosted node should be bound to a single authenticated owner, and CP must verify node_owner == the connecting identity, not trust the self-reported value). Likely: per-node keypair generated on the node; CSR/enrollment signed by Spawnery CA for cloud nodes; mTLS or signed-Register-token on the Attach stream; CP rejects class=cloud without a valid Spawnery-CA-chained cert; CP derives node_owner from the authenticated identity for self-hosted, not from the self-reported field. Interacts with E4 identity/secrets (sp-7h6) and the sp-gtm CP-key-vending MITM finding. DESIGN-FIRST. Until landed: document that NODE_CLASS/NODE_OWNER are trusted only in a closed all-Spawnery-operated deployment.
notes: MERGED TO MASTER 2026-06-10 (commit a8c9fb2). The node-auth/unified-identity impl (epic sp-g4w: PKI, AS/authsvc, mTLS dial, AS-signed sessions, client pin/verify, tenancy gate, enrollment) is now in mainline. Merge resolved server.go (kept master's token-based Register + reconcileInventory(sender) + upsertAgentCatalog, fed with sp-ova's VERIFIED nodeClass/nodeOwner) + registry_test.go (Heartbeat token-param signature). Default NODE_AUTH_MODE=insecure so runtime behavior unchanged. STILL OPEN for: the enforced-mode deployment decision + per-env Root CA (needs staging/prod). Unblocks owner-sealed-secrets delivery leg (sp-2ckv.2 sub-key signing/verify).

title: [bug] egress floor is in-pod-netns iptables — gVisor netstack only partially supports iptables; move floor to host veth
description: HOST-VERIFICATION RESEARCH (gvisor.md §4): our sp-rpa egress floor applies iptables INSIDE the container netns via nsenter. Under runc that's the real kernel netns (verified working). Under gVisor (runsc, netstack), the pod's netns is the Sentry's user-space TCP/IP stack which only PARTIALLY supports iptables — our DROP rules may silently behave differently / not enforce. Since we plan to adopt gVisor for the cloud tier, the floor must move OUT of the pod netns to the HOST: enforce on the host veth ingress hook / DOCKER-USER chain / CNI, where the real Linux netfilter sees gVisor packets exiting the veth normally. Affects firewall.NsenterApplier placement. Pre-req for gVisor adoption; also more robust under runc. Re-verify metadata+RFC1918 drop from the host side.
notes: EMPIRICALLY PROVEN on this host (runsc release-20260525.0). IN-NETNS floor (current NsenterApplier) under runsc = COMPLETE NO-OP: host-netns iptables DROP counters all 0 pkts, RFC1918 10.0.0.1 REACHABLE (vs blocked + 3-pkt counters under runc). gVisor netstack handles TCP/IP in user space + emits raw frames on the veth, so host-kernel netfilter in the container netns never sees workload traffic. => flipping on runsc TODAY would silently make the egress floor a no-op (metadata/RFC1918/exfil all open). HARD gVisor pre-req, P0. FIX VALIDATED: host DOCKER-USER rules matched by the pod's bridge source IP enforce under BOTH runtimes — under runsc, -s <containerIP> -d 169.254.0.0/16/-d RFC1918 DROP matched 3 pkts each, public reachable, dns :53 ACCEPT present. IMPL: get the SIDECAR container's bridge IP (docker inspect NetworkSettings.IPAddress — the netns owner; agent shares it via container:netns so no own IP); insert ordered DOCKER-USER rules (-s ip): udp/tcp 53 ACCEPT + EgressAllowCIDRs, then 169.254/16 + RFC1918 DROPs (insert reversed so final order is accepts-before-drops, or use a per-spawn child chain). NEW LIFECYCLE: DOCKER-USER persists across containers (unlike the auto-cleaned netns) — manager.Stop MUST remove this spawn's rules; track the IP per spawn. loopback (agent<->sidecar 127.0.0.1) is never forwarded so the host floor doesn't touch it. 172.16/12 drop is fine (blocks cross-container/internal; egress dest is external/public, allowed).
close_reason: Done+host-verified, merged b9f5342: egress floor moved from in-netns nsenter (PROVEN no-op under gVisor/runsc) to host DOCKER-USER chain matched by pod bridge IP (firewall.HostFloorApplier + Rules(ip); ContainerIP). Apply-on-create fail-closed, REMOVE-on-stop (FloorIP tracked). Shipped egress_e2e PASSES as root (metadata+RFC1918 dropped, public reachable) under runc AND validated equivalent under runsc. Unblocks gVisor adoption.

title: DEMO: automated App-review scanner is a committed demo build item
description: Open third-party publishing was chosen for the demo, so the E8 §5 LLM-as-judge App-review scanner (prompt-injection / data-exfiltration / jailbreak / scope-sanity over manifest+persona+skills) is NO LONGER deferred -- it gates every open publish and is the thing that makes open publishing safe. Real ML/eval build item. Run it OFF the DeepSeek inference GPU (sp-iui). Tune against curated red-team prompts + the seed apps. Affects E8 §5, E5 open-registry gate.
notes: REFRAMED via trust tiers (user decision): apps carry a per-version trust tier — unverified (published, not scanned) / scanned (passed this automated scanner) / reviewed (human). Runtime restrictions + user-facing warnings SCALE with tier; the enforced sandbox floor (egress + isolation + per-app consent) applies to ALL tiers. So the scanner is the 'scanned' tier mechanism, NOT a perfect single gate — a scanner miss is contained by the floor + the tighter unverified-tier sandbox + informed-consent disclosure. This de-risks the scanner's load-bearingness. Tier drops to 'scanned' on new version; permission escalation drops 'reviewed'. Seed apps ship 'reviewed'. Spec: E5 §5, E8 §5, scope §4.

title: DEMO-ROAST: threat-model inversion — user-steered agent IS the untrusted vector
description: The demo deferred egress enforcement (sp-ba5) and chose PLAIN CONTAINERS (no gVisor/microVM), both justified by 'no arbitrary creator code; Spawnery-vetted runtime only'. FALSE for the demo: the spawn runs a coding-style agent with shell/file/network tools, steered turn-by-turn by an untrusted free-tier stranger. The USER is the untrusted-code path. Consequences: open SSRF/egress-proxy/metadata-cred-theft by design (no egress allowlist), and container-escape/compute-abuse surface (plain containers + shell-capable agent). The security machinery cut for B+Y was load-bearing for B+Y too. FIX: treat the demo's spawn as hostile; pull egress floor (D2) + isolation hardening + resource limits (D3) + restricted toolset (D6) into the demo. Affects E1 isolation decision, E0/E8 egress, demo-mvp-scope.
notes: USER DECISION 2026-06-01: restricted agent toolset is POST-MVP/backlog. Strategy = allow-all tools by default; rely on REVIEW/AUDIT mechanisms (app-review scanner sp-5a9 + E8 audit) to detect abuse; escalate to VM/microVM isolation in the CLOUD if the platform takes off (not gVisor-per-spawn now). The OTHER sp-eha pieces ARE shipped: egress floor (sp-rpa), per-spawn cgroup limits + per-user quota + optional gVisor runtime knob (sp-ach). So sp-eha's hardening floor is substantially in place; only the toolset-restriction sub-item is deferred. Abuse-control for the demo now leans on sp-5a9 (scanner) + egress+limits floor + trust tiers + informed consent.

title: DEMO-ROAST: ship a hard egress allowlist floor in the demo (not post-MVP)
description: Egress enforcement was deferred as 'permissions/consent' (sp-ba5), but the per-spawn EGRESS FLOOR (a hard default, not per-app consent UI) is cheap and closes most network abuse. FIX: per-spawn network policy blocking everything except the localhost model sidecar + GitHub API/host + any app-declared domains; explicitly DROP 169.254.169.254 (cloud metadata) + RFC1918 internal ranges. Owner = E1 (pod netns). This is the single highest value/cost fix in the demo. Closes SSRF, egress-proxy, metadata-cred-theft, arbitrary-host exfil.
notes: CONFIRMED demo-required: egress floor is mandatory for the open marketplace.
close_reason: Egress block-floor implemented + merged 1435585: firewall pkg (Rules block-floor: drop 169.254/16 + RFC1918, allow lo + operator CIDRs, default-allow public; NsenterApplier), runtime.ContainerPID, manager.Create applies fail-closed BEFORE agent starts, node-class aware (cloud always enforces non-disableable; self-hosted honors EgressEnforce; default cloud). Hermetic tests (rule ordering, fail-closed, cloud-forces, self-hosted-opt-out) green; final review APPROVE. CAVEAT: real packet-drop UNVERIFIED in dev sandbox (no iptables/root) — build-tagged egress_e2e must run on node host (see sp-* follow-up).

title: ROAST: privacy/brand claims dishonest as written — reframe
description: Found by 2/4 (security #2/#3/#7, product F1-F5/F7). Multiple headline claims are weasel-worded for the cloud/free path: (a) 'E2E / CP never sees content' — channel terminates at the NODE, which on cloud IS Spawnery and audits content; (b) 'BYO key never reaches us' — on cloud nodes the node decrypts the plaintext BYO key + audits content; (c) 'your data stays yours' — CP holds GitHub-App signer + can mint write tokens to your repo silently; (d) 'one-click' — see ROAST-6. Reframe all copy: 'encrypted in transit to the Spawnery-operated node that runs your agent; on cloud, content is audited for abuse.' Reserve absolute privacy claims for self-host only. Affects system-design §1/§6/§7/§9.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: CP is total SPOF + relay bandwidth cliff
description: Found by ops #1/#2. The single central CP relays ALL non-LAN-direct session ciphertext (every token, every turn) AND mints tokens AND schedules. A CP restart/deploy/OOM drops every active conversation simultaneously and blocks new sessions = 100% cloud blast radius. No HA/drain/reconnect story. Relaying all conversation bytes = 2x egress through one box, saturates on bandwidth + connection count long before compute. FIX: split a stateless horizontally-scaled relay dataplane from control logic; session-resumption tokens (reconnect without re-auth); blue/green relay; prioritize LAN-direct/BYO-ingress off the post-MVP shelf; publish SLO + client reconnect behavior. Affects E0 §1/§10, E1.

title: ROAST: BYO-on-cloud audit has no inspection point — mechanical gap
description: Found by 2/4 (arch #1, security #3). Four docs assert 'BYO-on-cloud is still audited' but NO component sees BYO model traffic: the sidecar calls the provider directly (E2 §1), the node only terminates the transport channel + forwards ACP frames over loopback (not model prompts/responses), and the E8 classifier is scoped to 'Spawnery-operated inference' which BYO bypasses. FIX: make the SIDECAR the single audit/classifier point (it's the only component seeing all model traffic, managed + BYO; it's a Spawnery-controlled container). Reconcile every 'node is the audit point' phrasing to the sidecar. Affects E2 §1/§4, E8 §1/§2, E0 §9/§10, system §3.

title: ROAST: home-machine singleton + no DR/failover
description: Found by ops #3/#4. (a) One physical box runs the whole free tier + DeepSeek on one GPU; realistic ceiling ~5-20 concurrent generations; if it dies the free tier is hard-down (burst triggers on OVERLOAD from a live heartbeat, not on a DEAD node). (b) No backup/DR for CP state (spawn index, catalog, GitHub App signing JWT, OAuth refresh tokens, vault wraps, audit store) — DB loss = users can't find spawns, must re-auth all storage, vault ciphertext gone. FIX: active/standby or dead-node->cloud failover (not just overload->burst); decouple inference GPU pool from container CPU pool; CP DB backup/PITR/geo-replication; GitHub App key + OAuth secrets in KMS not the DB; document what's reconstructable from spawn.yml. Affects system §1, E1 §5, E0.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: CP key-vending enables trivial MITM — 'E2E' unverifiable
description: Found by 3/4 reviewers (security #1/#2, ops #10, product F3). The client receives the node static pubkey FROM the CP with no out-of-band verification (deferred post-MVP). A malicious/compromised CP substitutes its own key, runs key agreement as the client, decrypts BYO keys + full conversation, re-encrypts to the real node. Classic unauthenticated-DH key substitution. Worse: Spawnery is simultaneously CA + relay + key-directory, so even cert pinning doesn't stop the operator minting a node cert. The 'CP relays only opaque ciphertext / never sees content' claim is structurally false against the operator. FIX: vend full CA-signed cert chain + pin Spawnery CA root in the client bundle (not fetched at runtime); make out-of-band/TOFU node-key pinning MVP (esp. self-host); add key-transparency log. Affects E0 §10, E4 §6/§8, E6 §2.
notes: NARROWED 2026-06-11 by the auth epic (sp-ussy, 2026-06-11-auth-identity-design.md §5): client-signed intents (PoP on SessionOpen + the four StartSpawn-causing ops) moved into the auth epic. What remains here: E2E relay PAYLOAD encryption (agent stream confidentiality through a compromised CP).

title: Cross-component APIs & contracts
description: The shared seams every component depends on. Defines: spawneryapp.yml manifest schema; spawn.yml schema (data-repo source of truth, secrets excluded); container orchestration interface (start/exec/stop); ACP client<->agent orchestration contract; storage-adapter interface (materialize/persist); model-sidecar OpenAI-compatible API + central-gateway protocol + metering/audit hooks; CP index API; edge-activator session-token/auth protocol; permission/consent + egress-allowlist schema; catalog/registry index API. Design-first; all other epics consume these contracts. See design doc sections 2,3,5,6,7,8,9,10.
notes:
Design doc (v1, interview complete): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-26-spawnery-e0-contracts-design.md — see Appendix decision log (E0.1–E0.9).
v2 corrections: CP = single central managed instance (self-host = node agents attaching to it); rendezvous relay in CP; s2s=gRPC; Apps agent-agnostic (agent chosen at spawn, manifest declares compat); skills imported via agent's native process; storage detail -> E3; personalization + permissions -> post-MVP (TODO.md). Amended system-design §1/§4/§10.

title: Runtime & orchestration core
description: Control plane, node agent, container lifecycle (ephemeral, scale-to-zero), ACP orchestration layer, pluggable isolation-backend abstraction (concrete impls deferred), scheduler/placement (local-first, audit-flag set at placement). Design doc sections 3,4.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-27-spawnery-e1-runtime-core-design.md. Decisions: per-agent base image + mount definition (no per-(App,agent) build); cold-start every wake (warm pools post-MVP); placement = open app + owner node -> owner node else cloud; 2-D burst (CPU->offload containers, GPU->offload inference); idle/close/cap teardown, resume from /data; pod = base[agent+bridge+toolset]+sidecar; Docker/Podman backend first. Refined E0 §7/§3.

title: A5: web client auth — login UI, silent refresh, session keypair, intent signing
description: Spec §2,§5: login screen + OAuth redirect handling; access token in memory only + silent /refresh (HttpOnly AS-scoped cookie); non-extractable P-256 session keypair in IndexedDB; intent signing for SessionOpen + spawn lifecycle ops; Go<->TS vectors for intent bytes + SPKI hashing; Playwright login->spawn->attach vs fake provider. Files: web/src/api/, web/src/views/.
notes: ROASTED 2026-06-12: storage.persist() at key creation; positive key-can-sign self-check BEFORE /refresh; key missing -> revoke family + clean re-login; 90d family max age; distinct cnf-mismatch error code + recovery UX (AM6); Web Locks single-flight + jitter (AM3); pending-intent poll-and-sign for migrate/resume (AM1); first-run = hard login wall, no anonymous RPCs, state carries original route; multi-account = single per profile (family supersession); cross-origin /refresh Playwright incl cold reload (AM2).

title: A4: node-verified sessions + cnf-bound intent signatures
description: Spec §5: AS session pubkey joins node enrollment bundle; SessionOpen + CreateSpawn/ResumeSpawn/RecreateSpawn/MigrateSpawn intents carry access token + session-key signature over intent bytes; node offline verification chain (AS sig -> exp/aud -> owner match -> cnf -> intent sig -> freshness+jti); self-hosted keeps owner==NodeOwner; migration resume-leg fresh-token re-supply from live client; NODE_AUTH_MODE gating; host-gated e2e: enforced node refuses unsigned StartSpawn. Files: proto/node/v1, proto/cp/v1, internal/node/, internal/cp/lifecycle.go, internal/authsvc/enroll.go.
notes: ROASTED 2026-06-12 — AC1 IS THE CRITICAL: SignedIntent artifact (domain tag spawnery/intent/<op>/v1, two-phase sign-after-resolve echoing CP-committed {spawn_id, generation, target_node_id, image digest}, raw signed bytes travel verbatim in new auth envelope on StartSpawn/SessionOpen + full DER SPKI, node does field-by-field correspondence check); freshness 90s +-30s skew named constants, jti cache survives restarts, cross-node fan-out closed by target binding; session_id inside signed open; generation exposed on SpawnSummary; client-driven pending-intent poll-and-sign leg — clients NEVER sign unpended CP-supplied bytes (AM1); NACK codes through Connect errors/WS close; revocation-driven WS termination (AM10); dev parity: insecure = verify-and-log, dev clients always sign, hermetic 4-handler threading test (AM12, sp-gzvo class); delete internal/sessiontoken (MC1); P1363<->DER conversion at WebCrypto boundary + Go<->TS vectors (AM11).

title: A3: spawnctl login — loopback PKCE + headless paste
description: Spec §2: spawnctl login (browser to AS authorize, 127.0.0.1:<random> callback, token exchange); headless one-time-code paste fallback; tokens + session keypair in state dir 0600; transparent refresh on expiry. Files: cmd/spawnctl/.
notes: ROASTED 2026-06-12: single-flight advisory file lock + atomic rename on state file + refresh jitter (AM3); RFC 8628 device-grant polling replaces paste-a-code (AM7); session key = ECDSA P-256 like all clients (AM11); recorded residual: state dir holds cnf key + refresh token together (keychain = tracked improvement); automation = bootstrap + sliding family, never seed into ephemeral CI.

title: A2: CP offline token verification
description: Spec §4: internal/cp/auth swaps token-map for offline Ed25519 verify (sig over raw body bytes, exp, aud) -> WithOwner(accountId); WS in-band bind same path; pinned AS session pubkey via config (never fetched from AS); CP_DEV_TOKENS dev-mode only; CORS finalization (WL5). Files: internal/cp/auth/, cmd/spawnery_cp, internal/cp/ws.go.

title: Independent web SPA delivery (pinned static origin / AS-served, signed + SRI + strict CSP)
description: ROAST C2: SPEC B's web custody guarantee REQUIRES the SPA be delivered independently of the CP — that channel does not exist (no CSP/SRI/static-origin in web/; sp-ova §7/§9 open residual). A CP-served SPA = malicious JS uses the live device key to exfiltrate plaintext, defeating E2E for web. Needs its own design spec. Blocks sp-2ckv.3 (web device keys). Until it lands, custody guarantee is native-clients-only. Also resolves sp-ore.
design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-11-web-epic-spa-delivery-device-keys-migration-design.md §1 (slice W1). Canonical pinned static origin (header-capable host), SRI stamping, strict CSP w/ connect-src pinned to hosted CP+AS, cosign-keyless sign->verify->deploy gate, CP CORS pinning, Playwright vs CSP-enforced prod build in CI.
notes: ROASTED 2026-06-11 (2026-06-11-web-epic-adversarial-review.md): amendments folded into spec §1 — WS-upgrade Origin allowlist (WM18, replaces ws.go OriginPatterns *), CSP enumerated vs real bundle: font-src, sonner CSS, xterm styles, external theme script (WM19), npm ci + dep-tree residual honesty (WM20), falsifiable release gate + pinned cosign identity/issuer + anti-rollback + digest-bound build-test-deploy (WM21, WL2, WL3), dynamic-import SRI coverage + _headers in signed dist (WL4), localhost CORS dev-only (WL5), AS CORS + Connect-JSON wire (WL6), pinned RootCA+AS pubkeys compiled into bundle (WM8), public-DNS flip gated on minimal per-account auth (WM17).

title: Phase ④: fingerprint-bound enrollment tokens (amends sp-ova §3.1)
description: Enrollment tokens scoped to (accountId, class, node-pubkey-fingerprint, expiry, single-use); issued by AS to owner client over direct pinned connection; redeemed node→AS with CSR (AS checks CSR key matches bound fingerprint). Lands with/after worktree-node-auth-sp-ova merges. Spec §5.
close_reason: DONE + race-validated (master). AS enrollment tokens scoped to (accountId,class,node-pubkey-fingerprint,expiry,single-use): redemption recomputes SPKI fingerprint from CSR key, rejects substituted key (failed check doesn't burn the token); single-use + TTL + class/account scoping. internal/pki/fingerprint.go, authsvc bound-token flow, nodeid key persist, spawnlet enforced path. Backward-compat: legacy unbound path retained (follow-up to remove). 10 new tests + existing green.

title: Phase ③: swap consumers onto the primitive (journal keys, secrets store, BYOK)
description: Replace the transient-tier interim CP-custodied KeyProvider with owner-sealed delivery (this IS sp-u53.5.4 — close both together); wire sp-7h6.1 secrets store onto the envelope store; sidecar BYOK key rides the node leg at pod start. Deletes the CP-readable interim path. Spec §7.
notes: BLOCKED on proto regen (sealed-secret CP relay message) + sidecar tmpfs injection pod-wiring. Owner-sealed journal-key cutover (sp-u53.5.4) rides this.

Owner→CP(ciphertext-only relay)→node OpenDelivered→tmpfs 0600 at target_path; CP caches node sub-key+cert chain, never unseals; per-spawn secrets tmpfs at /run/spawnery/secrets (never-persist §6). version/deliveryId AAD = follow-up hooks for sp-u53.5.4. NOTE: the JOURNAL-key consumer swap (journal repo password over this path) is sp-u53.5.4.

title: Phase ②: web device keys + multi-device re-seal + recovery + fallback
description: WebCrypto non-extractable X25519 CryptoKey in IndexedDB (noble HPKE polyfill for envelope ops); QR/link device-add re-seal flow; BIP-39 recovery code as always-enrolled virtual device; Argon2id fallback KEK (client-side, params pinned in signed client code); AS device-set registry (owner-signed pubkey set; CP never holds trust). Spec §1,§4.
design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-11-web-epic-spa-delivery-device-keys-migration-design.md §2 (slice W2 = core only; device-management UI split to its own task). Web-first lazy ceremony, BIP-39 recovery virtual device, AS-stored hash-chained device set RPCs, enrollment link/QR + approve from web or spawnctl, re-seal engine, refuse-extractable, cross-language Go<->TS test vectors. Argon2id fallback + ephemeral device class DEFERRED (separate beads).

title: Phase ①: node HPKE sub-key + envelope + delivery leg (CLI-first, single-device)
description: Node: generate X25519 HPKE sub-keypair, publish pubkey in cert-key-signed expiring structure (72h, rotate at half-life); client verify chain: pinned roots → SAN → sub-key sig → expiry. HPKE-everywhere envelope (DEK + per-recipient HPKE Base seals; AAD at rest (accountId,secretId,version)); delivery re-seal with AAD (spawnId,generation,nodeId,notAfter). Single-device owner key in spawnctl (crypto/ecdh, 0600 keyfile). Spec §1-3. Libs: CIRCL or crypto/hpke.
notes: DELIVERY-HALF MERGED to master (fc33b04): internal/secrets/subkey — SignedSubKey (node-cert-ECDSA-P256-signed X25519 HPKE sub-key, 2-retained-half rotation + trial-Open), VerifyNodeForSealing (pinned-root+SAN via clientverify, revocation hook, sub-key sig+expiry), SealForNode/OpenDelivered. 11 tests green. sp-2ckv.2 library layer COMPLETE. REMAINING (wave 2, shared-file): CP relay proto for sealed-secret delivery + sidecar tmpfs injection + spawnctl device-key CLI — tracked, needs proto/manager coordination.
close_reason: Library + CLI DONE (master fc33b04 + 7d0584c). internal/secrets/seal (HPKE envelope, BIP-39 device keys, hash-chained member-signed device set, re-seal+AAD; M2/M4/M11 vectors), internal/secrets/subkey (cert-signed node HPKE sub-key + VerifyNodeForSealing pinned-root/SAN/revocation/expiry + 2-retained-half rotation), spawnctl key init/show/device-set/recover. The remaining transport — CP relay of sealed secrets to a live node + sidecar tmpfs injection — is proto-dependent (see sp-2ckv.4 + new proto issue).

title: Brainstorm + design spec: owner-sealed secret delivery

notes: Both research runs complete + merged. Combined results: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-owner-sealed-secrets-research-results.md (Merged synthesis + Design-spec inputs); cloud report imported verbatim alongside. Net: per-device X25519 keypairs as custody root (BIP-39 virtual-device recovery, Argon2id fallback, PRF Tier-2 only); cert-signed expiring HPKE sub-key binds node encryption key to sp-ova mTLS identity (RFC 9345/Signal-prekey pattern); HPKE node leg with (spawnId,generation,nodeId,notAfter) AAD; age or HPKE multi-recipient owner envelope; fingerprint-bound single-use enrollment tokens (extends sp-ova §3.1); never-persist as the tested invariant (memguard = defense-in-depth only); headless seams surveyed, design deferred. NEXT: design questions + spec.
close_reason: Brainstorm complete. Research (2 runs, merged) + approved design spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-owner-sealed-secrets-design.md. Decisions: per-device X25519 custody root (BIP-39 recovery virtual device, Argon2id fallback, PRF Tier-2 deferred); HPKE everywhere (DEK multi-recipient wrapper); cert-signed 72h node HPKE sub-keys; AAD context binding in flight + at rest; AS-held device registry; fingerprint-bound enrollment tokens; never-persist as tested invariant; no escrow v1 (seams reserved). Phases ①-④ filed under sp-2ckv.

This is the OWNER-SEALED custody tier that enables cross-node migration / node-death survival: seal the SAME node-local Kopia password to owner keys (no repo re-encryption, sidesteps kopia#309). Depends on sp-2ckv.2 (node leg). Pairs with MigrateSpawn (sp-u53.5.3).

OwnerSealedCustody serves a delivered repo password via the merged SecretDelivery path (secret_id journal/<mount>); internal/secrets/journalkey (SealToOwner/ResealForNode), internal/cp/journalkeys (ciphertext store + owner-device-registry SEAM — real registry + Put/Get wired by the migrate slice sp-u53.5.3). title: Manager: per-pod control token + control port, wire into SidecarEnv
description: internal/spawnlet/manager.go: generate per-pod SIDECAR_CONTROL_TOKEN secret, pass via SidecarEnv; configure sidecar control port; ensure node can reach PodIP:controlPort. internal/runtime/pod.go if needed. cmd/sidecar reads the env.
design:
# sp-bp9w.3 — Manager: per-pod control token + control port, wire into SidecarEnv

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (or superpowers:executing-plans) to implement task-by-task. Steps use `- [ ]` checkboxes.

**Goal:** Generate a per-pod control bearer token and wire `SIDECAR_CONTROL_TOKEN` + `SIDECAR_CONTROL_ADDR` into the sidecar env at spawn creation, and store the token + node-reachable control URL on the in-memory Spawn so the node's SetModel handler (sp-bp9w.5) can POST to the sidecar.

**Architecture:** The sidecar control endpoint (sp-bp9w.2, internal/sidecar/override.go) already starts a second http.Server **iff** both `SIDECAR_CONTROL_TOKEN` and `SIDECAR_CONTROL_ADDR` are set, and cmd/sidecar/main.go already reads those two env vars — so this task is purely manager-side: mint the token, set the two env vars, and persist token + control URL on the Spawn record. No sidecar / cmd changes.

**Tech stack:** Go; crypto/rand + encoding/hex (same pattern as internal/spawnlet/server.go newID); hermetic unit tests via the existing fakePodBackend.

---

## FILE LIST (controller: decides parallelism)

- Modify: `internal/spawnlet/store.go` — add `ControlToken` + `ControlURL` fields to `Spawn`.
- Modify: `internal/spawnlet/manager.go` — mint token, derive control port/addr, add the two SidecarEnv vars, build control URL from PodIP, populate the new Spawn fields; add `newControlToken()` helper.
- Modify: `internal/spawnlet/manager_sandbox_test.go` — extend `fakePodBackend` to capture the `runtime.PodSpec` passed to `StartPod`.
- Create: `internal/spawnlet/manager_control_test.go` — hermetic tests for token generation, env wiring, and the stored control URL.
- (No change) `cmd/sidecar/main.go`, `internal/sidecar/override.go` — already read the env (sp-bp9w.2). `internal/runtime/pod.go` — `PodSpec.SidecarEnv` + `PodHandle.PodIP` already exist; no change.

## DECISIONS (the .5 implementer relies on these)

- **Control-addr binding = `0.0.0.0:<controlPort>` (option (a)).** Justification: `SIDECAR_CONTROL_ADDR` is part of `SidecarEnv`, which is built **before** `StartPod` returns the handle — so the pod IP is unknown at env-build time. The two clean options are (a) bind the control listener to `0.0.0.0:<controlPort>` (all interfaces in the pod netns, which includes the pod bridge IP the node reaches it on) vs (b) restart/re-inject the sidecar after the IP is known. (b) requires sidecar-restart machinery that does not exist and breaks the two-phase StartPod/StartAgent flow; (a) is one string with no new infra. Safety: the endpoint is bearer-token-gated (constant-time compare, override.go `authorized`) and the agent container cannot read the sidecar's env (separate container, shared netns only), so binding all interfaces does not expand the practical attack surface. The egress floor governs **outbound** pod traffic; the control listener is **inbound** from the node over the pod bridge. Call this out in the commit message.
- **Control port = `SidecarPort + 1`** (default 8081), derived in code — no new ManagerConfig field (YAGNI; SidecarPort already defaults to 8080).
- **New `Spawn` fields (exact names — sp-bp9w.5 reads these):**
  - `ControlToken string` — the per-pod bearer secret (also passed as `SIDECAR_CONTROL_TOKEN`).
  - `ControlURL string` — full node-reachable endpoint `http://<PodIP>:<controlPort>/control/model`. (Includes the path so .5 POSTs to it directly.)

---

## Task 1: Add Spawn fields for the control token + URL

**Files:**
- Modify: `internal/spawnlet/store.go:5-17` (the `Spawn` struct)

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the two fields to the Spawn struct**

In `internal/spawnlet/store.go`, add after the `Mode` field (keep existing fields unchanged):

```go
type Spawn struct {
	ID         string
	Generation uint64 // CP-assigned generation (0 standalone); labels the pod for reconcile/fencing
	SidecarID  string
	AgentID    string
	MountDirs  []string // host dirs backing this spawn's mounts (for Finalize)
	FloorIP    string   // pod bridge IP the egress floor was applied for (for Remove on Stop)
	PodIP      string   // pod bridge IP (for the CRI/runsc TCP ACP attach to podIP:acpPort)
	NetnsPath  string   // /proc/<sidecar-pid>/ns/net — the pod netns, for the runc-lane AttachACP
	SandboxID  string   // CRI backend: the pod sandbox id (for teardown); empty for Docker
	Status     string
	Mode       string // run mode (acp|tmux|served|""); selects the terminal-attach inner command

	// ControlToken is the per-pod bearer secret the sidecar's control endpoint requires
	// (passed to the sidecar as SIDECAR_CONTROL_TOKEN). The node's SetModel handler sends it.
	ControlToken string
	// ControlURL is the node-reachable sidecar control endpoint,
	// "http://<PodIP>:<controlPort>/control/model". Empty if the pod has no IP.
	ControlURL string
}
```

## Task 3: Failing tests for token + env wiring + control URL

**Files:**
- Create: `internal/spawnlet/manager_control_test.go`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing tests**

Create `internal/spawnlet/manager_control_test.go`:

```go
package spawnlet

import (
	"context"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

// envVal returns the value of "KEY=..." in env, and whether the key was present.
func envVal(env []string, key string) (string, bool) {
	prefix := key + "="
	for _, e := range env {
		if strings.HasPrefix(e, prefix) {
			return strings.TrimPrefix(e, prefix), true
		}
	}
	return "", false
}

func TestCreateWiresControlTokenAndAddr(t *testing.T) {
	fb := &fakePodBackend{}
	m := NewManagerWithBackend(fb, &fakeApplier{}, ManagerConfig{
		AgentImage: "a", SidecarImage: "s", DataRoot: t.TempDir(),
	})

	sp, err := m.Create(context.Background(), "spz", writeApp(t), "model", "", "", 0)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Create: %v", err)
	}

	if sp.ControlToken == "" {
		t.Fatal("ControlToken is empty; want a generated per-pod secret")
	}

	// SidecarEnv must carry both control vars, with the token matching the Spawn record.
	tok, ok := envVal(fb.podSpec.SidecarEnv, "SIDECAR_CONTROL_TOKEN")
	if !ok {
		t.Fatal("SIDECAR_CONTROL_TOKEN not in SidecarEnv")
	}
	if tok != sp.ControlToken {
		t.Fatalf("env token %q != Spawn.ControlToken %q", tok, sp.ControlToken)
	}

	addr, ok := envVal(fb.podSpec.SidecarEnv, "SIDECAR_CONTROL_ADDR")
	if !ok {
		t.Fatal("SIDECAR_CONTROL_ADDR not in SidecarEnv")
	}
	// Default SidecarPort 8080 -> control port 8081, bound on all interfaces (pod IP unknown at env-build time).
	if addr != "0.0.0.0:8081" {
		t.Fatalf("SIDECAR_CONTROL_ADDR = %q, want 0.0.0.0:8081", addr)
	}

	// The stored control URL targets the pod IP + control port so the node can reach the sidecar.
	if sp.ControlURL != "http://10.0.0.5:8081/control/model" {
		t.Fatalf("ControlURL = %q, want http://10.0.0.5:8081/control/model", sp.ControlURL)
	}
}

func TestCreateControlTokenUniquePerSpawn(t *testing.T) {
	m := NewManagerWithBackend(&fakePodBackend{}, &fakeApplier{}, ManagerConfig{
		AgentImage: "a", SidecarImage: "s", DataRoot: t.TempDir(),
	})

	sp1, err := m.Create(context.Background(), "sp1", writeApp(t), "model", "", "", 0)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Create sp1: %v", err)
	}
	sp2, err := m.Create(context.Background(), "sp2", writeApp(t), "model", "", "", 0)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Create sp2: %v", err)
	}
	if sp1.ControlToken == sp2.ControlToken {
		t.Fatalf("control tokens not unique: both = %q", sp1.ControlToken)
	}
}
```

Run: `go test ./internal/spawnlet/ -run 'TestCreateWiresControlTokenAndAddr|TestCreateControlTokenUniquePerSpawn' -count=1`
Expected: FAIL — `ControlToken is empty` / `SIDECAR_CONTROL_TOKEN not in SidecarEnv` (fields/env not yet populated).

## Task 4: Implement token mint + env wiring + control URL in Create

**Files:**
- Modify: `internal/spawnlet/manager.go` — imports (add `crypto/rand`, `encoding/hex`), the `addr :=` block (~287), the `SidecarEnv` slice (~293), and the `sp := &Spawn{...}` line (~343); add `newControlToken()` helper near the bottom.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the crypto imports**

In `internal/spawnlet/manager.go` import block, add `"crypto/rand"` and `"encoding/hex"` (keep the rest):


- [ ] **Step 2: Mint the token and derive the control addr (before StartPod)**

Replace the `addr := ...` line (currently `addr := fmt.Sprintf("127.0.0.1:%d", m.cfg.SidecarPort)`) with:

```go
	addr := fmt.Sprintf("127.0.0.1:%d", m.cfg.SidecarPort)
	// Per-pod control plane: a random bearer token gates the sidecar's /control/model endpoint,
	// which the node POSTs to in order to switch the live model (runtime model switch, sp-bp9w).
	// SIDECAR_CONTROL_ADDR binds 0.0.0.0 (not loopback) because the pod IP is unknown until StartPod
	// returns, and the node reaches the sidecar over the pod bridge IP; the bearer token (not the
	// bind scope) is the access control, and the agent container cannot read the sidecar's env.
	controlToken := newControlToken()
	controlPort := m.cfg.SidecarPort + 1
	controlAddr := fmt.Sprintf("0.0.0.0:%d", controlPort)
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Add the two env vars to SidecarEnv**

Replace the `SidecarEnv` slice in the `StartPod` call:

```go
		SidecarEnv: []string{
			"OPENROUTER_API_KEY=" + m.cfg.OpenRouterKey,
			"SIDECAR_ADDR=" + addr,
			"SIDECAR_CONTROL_TOKEN=" + controlToken,
			"SIDECAR_CONTROL_ADDR=" + controlAddr,
		},
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Build the control URL and store both on the Spawn**

After the egress-floor block, before `m.store.Put(sp)`, replace the `sp := &Spawn{...}` line with:

```go
	// Node-reachable control endpoint (pod IP + control port). Empty PodIP => unreachable URL;
	// the reconciler/node handler treats that as "no live control plane".
	controlURL := ""
	if h.PodIP != "" {
		controlURL = fmt.Sprintf("http://%s:%d/control/model", h.PodIP, controlPort)
	}
	sp := &Spawn{ID: id, Generation: generation, SidecarID: h.SidecarID, AgentID: h.AgentID, MountDirs: mountDirs, FloorIP: floorIP, PodIP: h.PodIP, NetnsPath: h.NetnsPath, SandboxID: h.SandboxID, Status: "ready", Mode: sel.Mode, ControlToken: controlToken, ControlURL: controlURL}
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Add the newControlToken helper**

Add at the end of `internal/spawnlet/manager.go`:

```go
// newControlToken returns a 256-bit random hex string used as the sidecar control-endpoint
// bearer token (one per pod). Mirrors the crypto/rand+hex pattern in server.go's newID.
func newControlToken() string {
	b := make([]byte, 32)
	_, _ = rand.Read(b)
	return hex.EncodeToString(b)
}
```

## Self-review

- **Spec coverage:** Control path #3 — token via SidecarEnv (`SIDECAR_CONTROL_TOKEN`) ✓; control addr via SidecarEnv (`SIDECAR_CONTROL_ADDR`) ✓; node reaches `PodIP:<controlPort>/control/model` ✓ (stored as `ControlURL`); per-pod secret minted with crypto/rand ✓. Sidecar override mechanism — endpoint already implemented in sp-bp9w.2; this task only feeds it. Downstream coupling for sp-bp9w.5 — `Spawn.ControlToken` + `Spawn.ControlURL` populated ✓.
- **Ordering resolved:** token minted before StartPod (goes into env); control URL built after StartPod (needs PodIP). Bind scope `0.0.0.0` decouples env-build from the unknown pod IP.
- **Type consistency:** `newControlToken()`, `Spawn.ControlToken`, `Spawn.ControlURL`, `controlPort`, `controlAddr` used consistently across tasks. Test field `fakePodBackend.podSpec` matches Task 2.
- **No placeholders:** every code/command step is concrete.
- **Out of scope (do NOT do here):** node SetModel handler/POST (sp-bp9w.5), reconciler/DB `model_applied` (sp-bp9w.x), e2e (sp-bp9w.10).

design:
# Proto: SetSpawnModel RPC + SetModel/SetModelResult + codegen — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (or superpowers:executing-plans) to implement this task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax.

**Goal:** Add the wire surface for runtime model switching — a CP unary RPC and a CP<->node Attach-stream message pair — and regenerate Go protobuf/connect code. No behavior changes.

**Architecture:** Two hand-edited `.proto` files plus a single regen step. `SetSpawnModel` mirrors the existing `RenameSpawn` unary RPC on `SpawnService`. `SetModel`/`SetModelResult` ride the existing `Attach` bidi stream as new oneof arms on `CPMessage`/`NodeMessage`, generation-fenced like `StopSpawn`/`Suspend`. Generated code lands in `gen/` via `buf generate`.

**Tech Stack:** protobuf v3, buf v2 (`buf.gen.yaml` -> protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-connect-go, `paths=source_relative`), Go.

---

## Files

- Modify: `proto/cp/v1/cp.proto`
  - Add `rpc SetSpawnModel(SetSpawnModelRequest) returns (SetSpawnModelResponse);` to `service SpawnService`.
  - Add `message SetSpawnModelRequest  { string spawn_id = 1; string model = 2; }`
  - Add `message SetSpawnModelResponse { string model = 1; bool applied = 2; }`
- Modify: `proto/node/v1/node.proto`
  - Add oneof arm `SetModel set_model = 9;` to `message CPMessage` (next free field number; current max is `close_session = 8`).
  - Add oneof arm `SetModelResult set_model_result = 8;` to `message NodeMessage` (next free field number; current max is `session_status = 7`).
  - Add `message SetModel       { string spawn_id = 1; uint64 generation = 2; string model = 3; }`
  - Add `message SetModelResult { string spawn_id = 1; bool ok = 2; string detail = 3; }`
- Regenerated (do NOT hand-edit — output of `buf generate`):
  - `gen/cp/v1/cp.pb.go`
  - `gen/cp/v1/cpv1connect/cp.connect.go`
  - `gen/node/v1/node.pb.go`
  - `gen/node/v1/nodev1connect/node.connect.go`

**Field-number verification (do this, do not assume):** before editing, re-read both protos and confirm the highest oneof field numbers. As of this plan: `CPMessage` ends at `close_session = 8` -> use `9` for `set_model`; `NodeMessage` ends at `session_status = 7` -> use `8` for `set_model_result`. If the current file differs, use the actual next free number in each oneof and note the discrepancy in the commit message.

**Codegen command:** `make gen` (runs `buf generate`; the `.make/gen` stamp is keyed on `proto/**/*.proto` + `buf.gen.yaml` + `buf.yaml`, so editing the protos triggers regen). If the stamp is stale and refuses to rebuild, run `buf generate` directly from the repo root.

## Task 1: Add SetSpawnModel RPC to cp.proto

**Files:**
- Modify: `proto/cp/v1/cp.proto`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the RPC to SpawnService.** Insert after the `RenameSpawn` line (line ~18) inside `service SpawnService { ... }`:

```proto
  rpc SetSpawnModel(SetSpawnModelRequest) returns (SetSpawnModelResponse);
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add the request/response messages.** Insert near the other spawn-mutation messages, right after the `RenameSpawnResponse` message (line ~71):

```proto
// Change the model an already-running spawn uses (persist + best-effort live push).
// applied = the live pod's effective model now matches `model` (false => saved but pending).
message SetSpawnModelRequest  { string spawn_id = 1; string model = 2; }
message SetSpawnModelResponse { string model = 1; bool applied = 2; }
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Lint the proto.** Run: `buf lint`
  Expected: no output (clean). If buf is unavailable, skip — `make gen` will surface errors.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit.**

```bash
git add proto/cp/v1/cp.proto
git commit -m "proto(cp): add SetSpawnModel RPC for runtime model switch"
```

---

## Task 2: Add SetModel/SetModelResult to node.proto

**Files:**
- Modify: `proto/node/v1/node.proto`

- [ ] **Step 1: Re-verify field numbers.** Confirm `CPMessage` oneof max field is `8` and `NodeMessage` oneof max field is `7`. Adjust the numbers below if they differ.

- [ ] **Step 2: Add the CPMessage oneof arm.** Inside `message CPMessage { oneof msg { ... } }`, after `CloseSession close_session = 8;`:

```proto
    SetModel      set_model      = 9;     // CP -> node: switch the running pod's effective model (generation-fenced)
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Add the NodeMessage oneof arm.** Inside `message NodeMessage { oneof msg { ... } }`, after `SessionStatus session_status = 7;`:

```proto
    SetModelResult  set_model_result = 8; // node -> CP: ack/result of a SetModel push
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Add the message definitions.** Append near the other CP->node control messages (after `CloseSession` message, line ~88):

```proto
// CP -> node: switch the effective model of the running pod (forwarded to the sidecar override).
// Generation-fenced like StopSpawn/Suspend: the node ignores a SetModel whose generation
// doesn't match the pod it currently holds.
message SetModel       { string spawn_id = 1; uint64 generation = 2; string model = 3; }
// node -> CP: result of applying a SetModel. ok=false carries a human reason in detail.
message SetModelResult { string spawn_id = 1; bool ok = 2; string detail = 3; }
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Lint the proto.** Run: `buf lint`
  Expected: clean. (Skip if buf unavailable.)

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit.**

```bash
git add proto/node/v1/node.proto
git commit -m "proto(node): add SetModel/SetModelResult on Attach stream for model switch"
```

title: Runtime model switching (sidecar override + reconciler)
description: Change the inference model of an already-running spawn seamlessly (same session, next request uses new model), driven from web + spawnctl, persisted in spawns.model as the new default. Mechanism: sidecar force-rewrites the model field at the per-pod inference chokepoint; CP persists + pushes to node over the Attach stream; node POSTs a token-gated sidecar control endpoint; a CP reconciler guarantees eventual application (model_applied flag). See spec for full design.

title: Backend selection + node capability detection (self-hosted Mac)
description: Detect/advertise the Apple backend on a self-hosted Mac node and select it (runtime knob, cf. CONTAINER_RUNTIME; node-class=self-hosted). Ensure the cloud tier is unaffected (stays Linux). Scheduler/placement aware that the Apple backend is self-hosted-only.

title: macOS pod networking + egress floor equivalent
description: Provide sidecar<->agent reachability and a default-allow egress block-floor equivalent to sp-rpa (which is Linux iptables in the sidecar netns) on macOS — via pf or VM-level network controls. Fail-closed on floor-apply error, matching the Linux lane.

title: Implement the macOS PodBackend (pod-as-one-sandbox)
description: Implement runtime.PodBackend (internal/runtime/pod.go) for macOS using the chosen mechanism. Pod-as-one-sandbox: run sidecar+agent so the agent reaches the sidecar on loopback WITHOUT relying on Linux netns sharing (single VM/sandbox or equivalent). StartPod/StartAgent/Attach/Stop parity with the Docker + CRI lanes.

title: Spike + design: choose the macOS isolation mechanism
description: Research/spike to pick the mechanism for running the Linux agent+sidecar pod under Apple-native sandboxing on Apple Silicon self-hosted nodes: Apple Containerization framework (per-container microVM) vs Virtualization.framework (custom microVM) vs Seatbelt/sandbox-exec (process-level). Evaluate: isolation strength, ability to run a 2-process pod in ONE sandbox (the shared-loopback constraint from sp-7k8/sp-vaw), networking + egress control, macOS/hardware min versions, perf/cold-start, licensing. Output: a design doc in docs/superpowers/specs/ (add to INDEX.md) + a decision.

title: [epic] Apple-native sandboxing for self-hosted macOS nodes (isolation backend)
description:
Add a macOS-native isolation backend so a self-hosted node running on Apple Silicon can run spawns with strong, OS-native sandboxing instead of Docker-in-a-Linux-VM. Implements the existing PodBackend seam (internal/runtime/pod.go:75) as a new backend selected on self-hosted Mac nodes; the cloud tier stays Linux (gVisor path, sp-7k8). Mechanism is TBD by the spike — candidates: Apple's Containerization framework (per-container microVM on Apple Silicon, WWDC'25), Virtualization.framework microVMs, or Seatbelt/sandbox-exec for process-level. SELF-HOSTED ONLY.

HARD CONSTRAINT carried from sp-7k8/sp-vaw: the current pod = two containers sharing one netns (agent joins the sidecar netns, reaches it on 127.0.0.1:8080). That model BREAKS under per-container isolation (each microVM/sandbox has its own netstack — loopback not shared), exactly as found for per-container gVisor. So this backend MUST use a pod-as-one-sandbox model (both sidecar+agent in one VM/sandbox, or an equivalent shared-network primitive) — not one sandbox per container.

Consult prior designs (docs/superpowers/specs/INDEX.md): E1 runtime core, gVisor results + sp-7k8, runsc-cri pod backend (pod-as-one-sandbox precedent), egress floor sp-rpa, resource limits sp-ach, node-class/scheduler routing. In scope for MVP (self-hosted Mac support).
notes: Apple Silicon only (arm64); spawns are Linux containers, so a Linux-capable mechanism (microVM) is the likely path. macOS egress floor needs a pf-based (or VM-level) equivalent of the iptables floor (sp-rpa is Linux/iptables). Backend selection ties to node-class=self-hosted + a runtime knob (cf. CONTAINER_RUNTIME). Spike should produce a design doc in docs/superpowers/specs/ (+ INDEX.md entry).

title: E2E: verify Codex spawn over sidecar/Responses
description: Manual e2e in the pod: spawn the codex-tui runnable, confirm it launches in tmux and routes through the sidecar to OpenRouter's Responses API with the spawn model active, and that switching models via Codex's /model picker works for OpenRouter ids. Verify the three risks: (1) OpenRouter Responses Beta is stateless -> disable_response_storage=true works, no previous_response_id dependency; (2) the global default MODEL (deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash) is served over Responses or pick a Codex-appropriate default for the test; (3) first-run onboarding skips ChatGPT OAuth / trust-folder prompt under tmux.
acceptance_criteria: Codex runs end-to-end through the sidecar; /model switching works; all three risks confirmed or filed as follow-ups
notes: VERIFIED on host (2026-06-08): real wire path codex(Responses) -> local sidecar passthrough -> OpenRouter /v1/responses confirmed with codex rust-v0.137.0 + the exact launcher config.toml. Risk#1 stateless: codex exec resume --last 2-turn recall works, store=false, previous_response_id=null, full-history replay (input tokens doubled) — disable_response_storage=true correct. Risk#2 models: deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash (default) + openai/gpt-4o-mini both work over Responses. Risk#3 onboarding: fully headless, no ChatGPT login/trust prompt. NOT exercised in-session: full docker image build + gVisor pod + node registration + web runnable-select + in-TUI /model picker UX (same mechanism as working claude-code path; binary URL/install pattern verified statically).
close_reason: Closed

title: [epic] Container filesystem perms — writable userspace, ephemeral rootfs, persist only data mounts
description: Revisit the filesystem permission + persistence model inside the spawn container: (1) the whole filesystem should be writable and userspace modifiable by the agent user (agent can install/modify freely); (2) only changes under persistent data mounts (the named StorageBackend mounts in /app) are retained; (3) everything else — rootfs writes, scratch, tmp — is ephemeral and discarded on spawn deletion. Mechanism: writable ephemeral rootfs (overlay upper / tmpfs discarded at teardown) layered with the per-mount persistent backends, with uid/ownership set so the agent user can write everywhere. Verify across the Docker/runc lane and CRI lane; coordinate with the gVisor/runsc isolation work (sp-7k8) and the egress floor. In scope for MVP.
notes: Today Scratch backend nukes its dir on Finalize (internal/storage/storage.go); rootfs writability + ownership for the agent user needs auditing in internal/runtime (docker_pod.go, cri/backend.go) + pod spec (pod.go). Persistence boundary = the named data mounts only.

title: [epic] Apple iCloud storage backend (git-bundle blob Backend impl)
description: Implement the Apple iCloud blob storage backend behind internal/storage.Backend, same git-bundle shape as Drive (E3 §2b): binding icloud:<id>; Prepare = download .bundle then clone; persist = bundle create --all, upload, swap. RISK/UNKNOWN to resolve early: iCloud Drive has no clean public REST API for arbitrary third-party file storage outside a CloudKit container — investigate the viable mechanism (CloudKit web services / iCloud Drive document storage / app-scoped container) and credential model before committing the persist path; capture the decision. In scope for MVP.
notes: internal/storage/storage.go. iCloud API access is the open question — spike first. Reuses the blob materialize/persist mechanics + CP OAuth-style credential custody once the access path is chosen.

title: [epic] Google Drive storage backend (git-bundle blob Backend impl)
description: Implement the Google Drive blob storage backend behind internal/storage.Backend. Per E3 §2b: binding gdrive:<id>; Prepare = download the spawn's .bundle from Drive then git clone from the bundle into the mount; Finalize/persist = git bundle create --all into a fresh .bundle, upload, then swap (overwrite binding). Auth via Google OAuth: client secret + per-user refresh token held at the CP; CP mints short-lived access tokens, relays to node, reactive refresh-on-401 (E3 §3). Second adapter shipped after GitHub. In scope for MVP (one of the demo's three storage options).
notes: internal/storage/storage.go. Human-readable mirror (vs opaque bundle) = post-MVP. Shares the OAuth credential-custody/refresh path with iCloud/OneDrive blob backends.

title: [epic] GitHub storage backend (git-native Backend impl)
description: Implement the GitHub-native storage backend behind internal/storage.Backend (Prepare/Finalize) — today only Scratch (ephemeral) exists. Per E3 §2a + data-mounts design: binding github:owner/repo; Prepare = git clone (or fetch if cached) into the mount host dir; Finalize/persist = semantic commits + git push on checkpoint. Auth via Spawnery's GitHub App with fine-grained per-repo installation tokens (never broad 'repo' scope), minted at the CP and relayed to the node; reactive refresh-on-401 over the node gRPC stream (E3 §3). CP provisions the destination repo (default private). In scope for MVP (MVP-primary storage adapter).
notes: internal/storage/storage.go (Backend interface; add GitHub impl). Credential/refresh: CP mints installation token from App JWT, relays in createSpawn cmd; node never persists. Non-fast-forward = LWW surfaced (E3). Pre-upgrade snapshot via git tag.

title: Tests + e2e for secret delivery and injection
description: Unit tests for the secrets model/API + vault encryption; e2e proving a secret set in the UI is delivered over the E2E channel and injected into the correct container at startup (GITHUB_TOKEN visible to the agent; inference key reaching the sidecar) with the CP only ever holding ciphertext.

title: GitHub token injection — wire OAuth GitHub App token into the spawn
description: Reuse the GitHub App OAuth from sp-7h6 (login + storage double-duty) to provision a GITHUB_TOKEN delivered + injected into the agent for git/gh operations inside the spawn. Token scoping + refresh considerations noted; minimal MVP path first.

title: Inject secrets into the right container at spawn startup
description: Route each delivered secret to its target at spawn start: inference keys + provider id -> sidecar env (ties to multi-provider epic); GITHUB_TOKEN and app secrets -> agent env; file-type secrets -> mounted files. Replace the single OpenRouterKey injection (manager.go:251) with this generalized binding. Define a per-spawn manifest declaring which secrets the spawn receives.

title: Deliver secrets to the node over the per-session E2E channel
description: At session/spawn start, deliver the spawn's secrets as AEAD control messages over the per-session E2E channel (E0 §10): client decrypts from vault, re-encrypts to the session key, CP relays opaque ciphertext, node decrypts. Generalizes the existing single-BYO-key delivery to N typed secrets. CP never sees plaintext.

title: Encrypt secrets at rest under the E4 vault (CP-blind)
description: Reuse the E4 vault (passphrase/VK, Argon2id, server-blind — sp-7h6) to encrypt secret values client-side before they reach the CP, so the CP stores only ciphertext. Decryption happens client-side (for delivery) / node-side (for injection).

title: Secrets data model + user-scoped CRUD API
description: User-scoped secrets store: typed entries (github-token, inference-key{provider}, generic kv) with a name, target hint (sidecar|agent-env|file), and value. CP-side CRUD API; values stored encrypted (CP-blind) — never plaintext at rest on the CP.

title: Tests + e2e across providers; deployment/docs env update
description: Unit tests for the provider abstraction + auth schemes; extend internal/sidecar tests; an e2e against >=1 additional provider beyond OpenRouter. Update deployment.md env table (replace OPENROUTER_API_KEY-only with provider+key vars) and README.

title: Generalize spawn-time wiring — pass provider+key instead of OpenRouterKey
description: Replace the single m.cfg.OpenRouterKey path (internal/spawnlet/manager.go:251) and the OPENROUTER_API_KEY env with a provider-selection + key plumbed through the pod specs (internal/runtime/pod.go, docker_pod.go, cri/backend.go) into the sidecar container env. Coordinate with the user-secrets epic for where the key/provider choice comes from (managed vs BYOK).

title: Add direct providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini behind the shared lib
description: Wire concrete direct-BYO providers through the provider abstraction: OpenAI (Chat Completions, Bearer), Anthropic (native Messages, x-api-key), Google Gemini (OpenAI-compat endpoint or path quirks + API key). Handle per-provider model id namespacing and any request/response quirks. Keep OpenRouter working as one provider among many.

title: Provider abstraction in the sidecar — config-driven base URL + auth scheme
description: Introduce a Provider abstraction in internal/sidecar: provider id, base URL, and auth scheme (Bearer 'Authorization', Anthropic 'x-api-key' + 'anthropic-version', Google API-key). Replace the hardcoded OpenRouter default in cmd/sidecar/main.go and the Bearer-only auth in anthropic.go/proxy.go with selection driven by env/config (e.g. SIDECAR_PROVIDER + SIDECAR_API_KEY + SIDECAR_UPSTREAM). Adding a provider becomes a table entry, not new code paths.

title: [epic] User secrets store — auto-inject at spawn startup (GitHub tokens, BYOK inference keys, etc.)
description: A user-scoped secrets store whose entries are automatically delivered and injected into a spawn at startup — e.g. GITHUB_TOKEN for git/gh inside the agent, BYOK inference keys for the sidecar, and arbitrary app secrets. Builds on the E4 vault + the per-session E2E BYO-key delivery channel (E0 §10 / docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-spawnery-e4-identity-secrets-design.md): secrets stored CP-blind, relayed as opaque ciphertext, decrypted by the node, injected into the right container (sidecar vs agent) as env/files at spawn start. Today only a single inference key is plumbed (OpenRouterKey -> sidecar env); there is no general user-secrets mechanism and no GitHub token injection. In scope for MVP.
notes: NOTE 2026-06-10: child tasks .2 (E4-vault encrypt-at-rest) and .3 (per-session E2E delivery) are SUPERSEDED by the sp-2ckv owner-sealed-secrets primitive (spec 2026-06-10-owner-sealed-secrets-design.md) — reconcile/close-as-dup when sp-2ckv.4 (consumer swap) executes; .1/.4/.5/.7 remain valid on top of the new primitive.

title: [epic] Multi-provider inference — generalize sidecar beyond OpenRouter (BYO + managed)
description: Today the model sidecar is hardwired to a single provider: cmd/sidecar/main.go defaults SIDECAR_UPSTREAM to https://openrouter.ai/api and reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY; spawnlet/manager.go:251 injects a single m.cfg.OpenRouterKey env. anthropic.go translates the Anthropic Messages API -> OpenAI Chat Completions (OpenRouter's surface) and everything else is OpenAI passthrough. This epic generalizes the sidecar + spawn wiring to support multiple inference providers (direct BYO and managed), per E2 design doc §5 / decision E2.7 (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-27-spawnery-e2-model-layer-design.md). Goal: adding a provider is config, not code surgery, and the agent can target OpenAI / Anthropic / Google / OpenRouter / local DeepSeek through the same loopback endpoint. In scope for MVP.
notes: Key call sites: cmd/sidecar/main.go (upstream+key bootstrap), internal/sidecar/anthropic.go (Anthropic<->OpenAI translation + auth header), internal/sidecar/proxy.go (passthrough), internal/spawnlet/manager.go:251 (key injection), internal/runtime/pod.go + docker_pod.go + cri/backend.go (sidecar env/spec), deployment.md §env. Related to user-secrets/BYOK epic for key delivery.
