Node launches/reaps N sessions in the running container: mosh = exec launcher -> uniquely-named detached tmux + per-session PTY relay; acp = lowest-free port 32668-32767 (reject on exhaustion) -> tmux-wrapped endpoint (acp-<id>) + Nth Pump dialing podIP:N. Sync id+port reservation on receive goroutine, async launch with mid-launch close re-check; closeSession reaps exactly one session (remove-before-teardown, ownership-checked port free) - no double-free, no orphaned pump. opencode-tui gated additional-only (requires opencode-served). sessionExec seam for unit tests (fake exec boundary; real docker/TCP paths -> sp-npxq.5 e2e). Spec+quality reviews pass (incl close-races-launch regression tests).

comments.author: Alexey Parfenov
comments.text: DESIGN DECISION carried from sp-npxq.2 review: opencode-tui now runs 'spawn-tui' (opencode attach) not standalone 'opencode', so an opencode-tui session needs a served opencode backend. spawn-tui now fails LOUD if none reachable. When wiring session-creation eligibility here, decide: gate opencode-tui to additional-session-only (attach to the spawn's existing served opencode), or keep it primary-eligible. Multi-session design implies opencode-primary = opencode-served (ChatView). Also: allocID() in attach.go does NOT reserve ids — keep CreateSession launch on the single Attach receive goroutine OR add reservation if you make launch async.

title: Reusable per-runnable launch/env (factor the entrypoint dispatcher)
description: Today the per-runnable launch + env tweaks live in deploy/agent/entrypoint.sh's case (opencode sidecar opencode.json + OPENCODE_CONFIG, goose OpenAI-sidecar env, claude apiKeyHelper + ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, tmux wrapping, acpmux for acp). Factor these into a reusable in-image launcher (e.g. a script the entrypoint AND exec-launched sessions both call with a runnable id) so an ADDITIONAL session started via docker exec gets identical config. Builds on sp-9xr.18's setup_opencode_provider factoring. Enables the node to launch any runnable into a running container consistently.
design:
# Reusable in-image launcher + transparent tmux (sp-npxq.2) — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Factor `entrypoint.sh`'s per-runnable launch/env into ONE in-image `launcher` script that BOTH the entrypoint (session #0) and (later, sp-npxq.3) the node via `docker exec` call, and wrap ALL mosh-transport runnables (opencode included) in a single baked transparent `tmux.conf`.

**Architecture:** New `/usr/local/bin/launcher` owns every per-runnable env tweak + launch decision (`acpmux`/`acpexec`/`acpadapter` for acp/served, transparent-tmux wrapping for mosh). `entrypoint.sh` becomes a one-line `exec launcher --runnable "$1" --keepalive`. The launcher `exec`s the foreground server for acp/served (PID 1 unchanged) and creates a detached transparent-tmux session for mosh; `--keepalive` (session #0 only) runs the keep-PID-1-alive loop that lived in `spawn-tmux`. A baked `/etc/tmux.conf` makes tmux ~invisible so opencode (and every TUI/shell) shares one tmux session = true multi-client mirroring.

**Tech Stack:** POSIX `sh`, tmux (Debian stable ≥3.3), Docker, Go (no Go code changes — only an awareness pass).

**Scope guardrails (do NOT cross):** NO node-side launch/exec code (that is sp-npxq.3), NO proto changes (sp-npxq.1), NO web changes (sp-npxq.4). Session #0 behavior MUST stay byte-identical: same ACP port (7000), same tmux session name (`spawn`), same env per runnable, acp/served still `exec` (PID 1 = the server, so SIGTERM handling is unchanged).

---

## Files

- **Create:** `deploy/agent/launch` — the reusable launcher script, installed as `/usr/local/bin/launcher`. Owns `setup_opencode_provider` (moved from entrypoint) + all per-runnable env/launch + transparent-tmux wrapping.
- **Create:** `deploy/agent/tmux.conf` — the baked transparent tmux config, installed to `/etc/tmux.conf`.
- **Modify:** `deploy/agent/entrypoint.sh` — collapse the per-runnable `case` to `exec launcher --runnable "${1:-}" --keepalive`; remove `setup_opencode_provider` (now in launcher).
- **Modify:** `deploy/agent/spawn-tui.sh` — rewrite the obsolete "no inner tmux" header comment (it is now the INNER command of a transparent-tmux session); attach logic unchanged.
- **Modify:** `deploy/agent/Dockerfile` — `COPY` the launcher + tmux.conf, `chmod +x` the launcher, add `ncurses-term` to the apt install (provides the `tmux-256color` terminfo entry required by `default-terminal`), and retire the now-unused `spawn-tmux` COPY/chmod.
- **Delete:** `deploy/agent/spawn-tmux` — its two jobs split into the launcher (create detached session) and the launcher's `--keepalive` loop. No Go code references it (only `entrypoint.sh` did; the node attaches via `tmux attach -t spawn`).

**No Go files change.** `internal/node/attach.go:293` and `internal/spawnlet/terminal.go:67` attach to the hardcoded tmux session name `spawn`; the launcher MUST keep creating session #0 as `spawn` so these keep working untouched.

---

## Reference: current behavior to preserve (read before editing)

`deploy/agent/entrypoint.sh` dispatches on `$1` (the runnable id the node sets as the container Cmd — see `internal/spawnlet/manager.go` `CreateWithSelection`, `agentCmd = []string{sel.RunnableID}`):

| `$1` (runnable)        | current action |
|------------------------|----------------|
| `""` / `opencode-served` | `setup_opencode_provider`; `opencode serve --port 4096 --hostname 127.0.0.1 &`; `export OPENCODE_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:4096`; `exec /usr/local/bin/acpadapter` |
| `opencode-tui`         | `export TERM`; `setup_opencode_provider`; `exec spawn-tmux opencode` |
| `goose-tui`            | export `GOOSE_PROVIDER/GOOSE_MODEL/OPENAI_API_KEY/GOOSE_TELEMETRY_OFF` + `TERM`; `exec spawn-tmux goose session` |
| `goose-acp`            | export goose env (same 4 vars); `exec /usr/local/bin/acpmux goose acp` |
| `claude-tui`           | export `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` (from `${OPENAI_BASE_URL%/v1}`), `ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_MODEL_OPTION(_NAME)`, `ANTHROPIC_MODEL`, `ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH`, `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER`, `DISABLE_TELEMETRY`, `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC`, `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_TERMINAL_TITLE` + `TERM`; `exec spawn-tmux claude` |
| `nori*`                | error: "nori is launched by the terminal attach, not the dispatcher"; `exit 1` |
| anything else          | error: "unknown runnable: $1"; `exit 1` |

`deploy/agent/spawn-tmux`: `tmux -u new-session -d -s "${SPAWN_TMUX_SESSION:-spawn}" -- "$@"` then `while tmux has-session -t "$SESSION"; do sleep 1; done` (keep-PID-1-alive). The node currently sets `ACP_LISTEN=tcp://0.0.0.0:7000` in `internal/runtime/docker_pod.go`; `acpmux`/`acpexec`/`acpadapter` read `ACP_LISTEN` directly (`deploy/agent/acpmux/main.go:110`).

**Key design decisions baked into this plan:**
1. **Launcher interface:** `launcher --runnable <id> [--acp-port <N>] [--tmux-session <NAME>] [--keepalive]`. Defaults: `--acp-port 7000`, `--tmux-session spawn`, keepalive off. `--keepalive` is session-#0-only (entrypoint passes it); it is the ONLY behavioral difference between session #0 and an exec session — the env/wiring is byte-identical.
2. **acp port threading:** launcher sets `export ACP_LISTEN=tcp://0.0.0.0:<acp-port>` for acp/served runnables. Default 7000 == what the node sets today, so session #0 is identical; sp-npxq.3 can later request a fresh port via `--acp-port`.
3. **acp/served still `exec`:** so PID 1 stays the server (`acpmux`/`acpexec`/`acpadapter`) and SIGTERM handling is unchanged. `--keepalive` is irrelevant on these paths because `exec` never returns. Keep `opencode-served` on `acpadapter` (NOT `acpexec`) — the spec §4 prose says "acpexec for opencode-served" loosely, but the live wiring is `acpadapter`; preserving byte-identical session #0 wins.
4. **Transparent tmux for ALL mosh runnables, opencode included:** launcher wraps each mosh runnable via a shared `start_tmux` helper: `tmux -u new-session -d -s "$NAME" -e VAR=val ... -- <inner argv>`. For `opencode-tui` the inner argv is `spawn-tui` (the existing opencode-attach script). Per-session env is passed with `tmux new-session -e` (tmux ≥3.0) so an exec session that joins session #0's already-running tmux **server** still gets the exact env, instead of inheriting the server's global env. For session #0 there is no pre-existing server, so `-e` yields identical values either way — it is correctness insurance for sp-npxq.3.
5. **Built-in `shell` runnable:** synthetic, transport mosh, inner command `bash`, transparent-tmux wrapped. Add a `shell)` case to the launcher now (the node will request it in sp-npxq.3). It needs no provider env — only `TERM`.

## Task 1: Bake the transparent tmux config

**Files:**
- Create: `deploy/agent/tmux.conf`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the tmux config**

Create `deploy/agent/tmux.conf` verbatim (these are the spec §3 settings; comments document intent):

```sh
# Spawnery transparent tmux: makes tmux ~invisible so every mosh-transport runnable
# (claude/goose/opencode TUIs + the built-in shell) renders as if there were no tmux,
# while still giving us one shared session per (spawn,session) for true multi-client
# mirroring and reconnect/scrollback persistence. Baked to /etc/tmux.conf (auto-loaded
# by every tmux server in the image). See sp-npxq spec §3.
set  -g  status off                    # no status bar
set  -g  prefix None                   # don't intercept C-b — all keys reach the app
unbind C-b
set -sg  escape-time 0                 # no ESC delay; TUIs feel native
set  -g  mouse off                     # app owns the mouse
set  -g  default-terminal "tmux-256color"
set -ga  terminal-overrides ",*:Tc"    # truecolor passthrough
set  -g  allow-passthrough on          # raw DCS (graphics/img protocols) passthrough
set  -g  set-clipboard on              # OSC 52 clipboard passthrough
set  -g  set-titles on                 # forward the app's terminal title
set  -g  visual-activity off
set  -g  visual-bell off
set  -g  monitor-activity off
set  -g  detach-on-destroy on          # agent exits → session ends (matches keepalive loop)
```

## Task 2: Create the reusable launcher script

**Files:**
- Create: `deploy/agent/launch`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the launcher**

Create `deploy/agent/launch` verbatim:

```sh
#!/bin/sh
# Reusable in-image launcher. Owns ALL per-runnable env + launch wiring, so session #0
# (the entrypoint) and additional sessions (the node via `docker exec`, sp-npxq.3) get
# byte-identical config. acp/served runnables exec a foreground server (PID 1 = server);
# mosh runnables get a detached transparent-tmux session (one tmux session per
# (spawn,session)), and --keepalive (session #0 only) blocks PID 1 until that session ends.
#
#   launcher --runnable <id> [--acp-port <N>] [--tmux-session <NAME>] [--keepalive]
#
# Defaults: --acp-port 7000, --tmux-session spawn, keepalive off. Defaults match what the
# node sets for session #0 today (ACP_LISTEN=:7000, tmux session "spawn"), so session #0 is
# unchanged. See sp-npxq spec §2/§3/§4.
set -e

RUNNABLE=""
ACP_PORT=7000
TMUX_SESSION=spawn
KEEPALIVE=0
while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --runnable)     RUNNABLE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --acp-port)     ACP_PORT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --tmux-session) TMUX_SESSION="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --keepalive)    KEEPALIVE=1; shift ;;
    *) echo "launcher: unknown arg: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
  esac
done

# setup_opencode_provider generates the opencode config that routes opencode (TUI or served)
# through the sidecar's OpenAI-compatible endpoint (custom "spawnery" provider -> SIDECAR_BASE,
# which injects the real OpenRouter key) and selects the spawn's model as "spawnery/<MODEL_ID>".
# Both the served path AND opencode-tui must call this — otherwise the TUI falls back to
# opencode's built-in provider (opencode zen) and bypasses the sidecar (sp-9xr.18). Exports
# OPENCODE_CONFIG and SPAWN_MODEL.
setup_opencode_provider() {
  SIDECAR_BASE="${SIDECAR_BASE:-http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1}"
  MODEL_ID="${SPAWN_MODEL:-openai/gpt-4o-mini}"
  mkdir -p /etc/opencode
  cat > /etc/opencode/opencode.json <<EOF
{
  "\$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "model": "spawnery/${MODEL_ID}",
  "provider": {
    "spawnery": {
      "npm": "@ai-sdk/openai-compatible",
      "name": "Spawnery Sidecar",
      "options": { "baseURL": "${SIDECAR_BASE}", "apiKey": "sk-unused-sidecar-injects-real-key" },
      "models": { "${MODEL_ID}": { "name": "${MODEL_ID}" } }
    }
  }
}
EOF
  export OPENCODE_CONFIG=/etc/opencode/opencode.json
  export SPAWN_MODEL="spawnery/${MODEL_ID}"
}

# start_tmux <inner-argv...> — create a detached transparent-tmux session named $TMUX_SESSION
# running the runnable, passing the runnable's env explicitly via -e (so an exec session that
# joins session #0's already-running tmux server still gets the exact env). Then, if
# --keepalive, block PID 1 while the session lives (this is what spawn-tmux used to do).
# Per-runnable env vars are exported by the caller and forwarded below via TMUX_ENV.
start_tmux() {
  # shellcheck disable=SC2086  # TMUX_ENV is intentionally word-split into -e KEY=VAL flags
  tmux -u new-session -d -s "$TMUX_SESSION" $TMUX_ENV -- "$@"
  if [ "$KEEPALIVE" = 1 ]; then
    # Keep PID 1 alive while the tmux session exists (clients attach via `tmux attach -t
    # <name>`). When the agent exits, its window closes, the session ends (detach-on-destroy),
    # and this loop returns so PID 1 exits and the node reaps the pod.
    while tmux has-session -t "$TMUX_SESSION" 2>/dev/null; do
      sleep 1
    done
  fi
}

# tmux_env adds "-e KEY=VAL" for each NAME passed, reading the value from the current env.
# Accumulates into TMUX_ENV (consumed by start_tmux).
TMUX_ENV=""
tmux_env() {
  for _n in "$@"; do
    eval "_v=\${$_n}"
    TMUX_ENV="$TMUX_ENV -e $_n=$_v"
  done
}

case "$RUNNABLE" in
  ""|opencode-served)
    # Default opencode serve + adapter. Same sidecar provider config as opencode-tui.
    export ACP_LISTEN="tcp://0.0.0.0:${ACP_PORT}"
    setup_opencode_provider
    # opencode listens on loopback; both clients (the adapter and the in-pod TUI) are in-pod.
    opencode serve --port 4096 --hostname 127.0.0.1 &
    export OPENCODE_BASE_URL="${OPENCODE_BASE_URL:-http://127.0.0.1:4096}"
    exec /usr/local/bin/acpadapter
    ;;
  goose-acp)
    # goose speaks ACP over stdio; acpmux fans N ACP clients onto ONE shared `goose acp`
    # (web via the node pump + in-container nori via acpdial). Same OpenAI-sidecar provider
    # config as goose-tui (model via the sidecar).
    export ACP_LISTEN="tcp://0.0.0.0:${ACP_PORT}"
    export GOOSE_PROVIDER="${GOOSE_PROVIDER:-openai}"
    export GOOSE_MODEL="${GOOSE_MODEL:-${SPAWN_MODEL:-openai/gpt-4o-mini}}"
    export OPENAI_API_KEY="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-sk-unused-sidecar-injects-real-key}"
    export GOOSE_TELEMETRY_OFF="${GOOSE_TELEMETRY_OFF:-1}"
    exec /usr/local/bin/acpmux goose acp
    ;;
  opencode-tui)
    export TERM="${TERM:-xterm-256color}"
    setup_opencode_provider   # same sidecar provider + model as opencode-served
    # opencode TUI has no session-title hook (SPAWN_SESSION_TITLE) — naming is best-effort;
    # the load-bearing fix is the sidecar routing above (sp-9xr.18). The inner command is
    # spawn-tui, now wrapped in transparent tmux so all clients mirror one TUI process.
    tmux_env TERM OPENCODE_CONFIG SPAWN_MODEL OPENCODE_BASE_URL SPAWNERY_SESSION_FILE
    start_tmux spawn-tui
    ;;
  goose-tui)
    export TERM="${TERM:-xterm-256color}"
    export GOOSE_PROVIDER="${GOOSE_PROVIDER:-openai}"
    export GOOSE_MODEL="${GOOSE_MODEL:-${SPAWN_MODEL:-openai/gpt-4o-mini}}"
    export OPENAI_API_KEY="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-sk-unused-sidecar-injects-real-key}"
    export GOOSE_TELEMETRY_OFF="${GOOSE_TELEMETRY_OFF:-1}"
    # OPENAI_BASE_URL is set by the node to the sidecar endpoint; goose's openai provider reads
    # it directly. Use "goose session" (bare "goose" opens the configure wizard).
    tmux_env TERM GOOSE_PROVIDER GOOSE_MODEL OPENAI_API_KEY GOOSE_TELEMETRY_OFF OPENAI_BASE_URL
    start_tmux goose session
    ;;
  claude-tui)
    export TERM="${TERM:-xterm-256color}"
    # Claude Code speaks the Anthropic Messages API; the sidecar's /v1/messages converter
    # translates to OpenAI Chat Completions against OpenRouter. Strip trailing /v1 for
    # ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (Claude Code appends /v1/messages itself). The dummy key is supplied
    # via the baked apiKeyHelper (/root/.claude/settings.json), NOT an env var (sp-9xr.18).
    SIDECAR_OAI="${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1}"
    export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL="${ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL:-${SIDECAR_OAI%/v1}}"
    export ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_MODEL_OPTION="${SPAWN_MODEL:-openai/gpt-4o-mini}"
    export ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_MODEL_OPTION_NAME="Spawnery"
    export ANTHROPIC_MODEL="${ANTHROPIC_MODEL:-${SPAWN_MODEL:-openai/gpt-4o-mini}}"
    export ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH="${ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH:-true}"
    export DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER="${DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER:-1}"
    export DISABLE_TELEMETRY="${DISABLE_TELEMETRY:-1}"
    export CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC="${CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC:-1}"
    export CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_TERMINAL_TITLE="${CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_TERMINAL_TITLE:-1}"
    tmux_env TERM ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_MODEL_OPTION ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_MODEL_OPTION_NAME \
             ANTHROPIC_MODEL ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER DISABLE_TELEMETRY \
             CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_TERMINAL_TITLE
    start_tmux claude
    ;;
  shell)
    # Built-in synthetic runnable: a plain login-ish bash shell, transparent-tmux wrapped so it
    # shares the same multi-client mirroring + scrollback persistence as the agent TUIs (sp-npxq §3).
    export TERM="${TERM:-xterm-256color}"
    tmux_env TERM
    start_tmux bash
    ;;
  nori*|nori)
    echo "nori is launched by the terminal attach, not the launcher (sp-9xr.12.2)" >&2; exit 1 ;;
  *)
    echo "launcher: unknown runnable: $RUNNABLE" >&2; exit 1 ;;
esac
```

## Task 3: Reduce entrypoint.sh to a launcher delegate

**Files:**
- Modify: `deploy/agent/entrypoint.sh` (replace the whole body)

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace entrypoint.sh with the thin delegate**

Overwrite `deploy/agent/entrypoint.sh` with:

```sh
#!/bin/sh
# Dispatcher entrypoint for session #0. The node passes the runnable id as $1 and sets the
# image Cmd to [runnableID] (internal/spawnlet/manager.go). All per-runnable launch/env wiring
# now lives in the reusable launcher (deploy/agent/launch), shared with the additional sessions
# the node exec-launches in sp-npxq.3 — so every session gets byte-identical config.
#
# Session #0 keeps ACP port 7000 (the node also sets ACP_LISTEN=:7000) and the tmux session
# name "spawn" (the node attaches via `tmux attach -t spawn`). --keepalive runs the
# keep-PID-1-alive loop for mosh runnables (acp/served exec a foreground server instead).
set -e
exec launcher --runnable "${1:-}" --acp-port 7000 --tmux-session spawn --keepalive
```

No code change in this task. If Step 1/2 produced no edits, there is nothing to commit — proceed to the session-close push protocol (pull --rebase, push, verify up-to-date).

---

## Self-review notes

- **Spec §2 (reusable launcher):** Task 2 — launcher owns opencode.json/OPENCODE_CONFIG/OPENCODE_BASE_URL, goose env, claude apiKeyHelper-adjacent env + ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, acpmux/acpadapter selection. Reuses `setup_opencode_provider` (moved, not rewritten). ✓
- **Spec §3 (transparent tmux, no exemptions):** Task 1 (config) + Task 2 (`start_tmux` wraps every mosh runnable incl. opencode-tui via spawn-tui, and shell). ✓
- **Spec §4 (configurable acp port):** launcher `--acp-port` → `ACP_LISTEN=tcp://0.0.0.0:<N>`, default 7000. ✓ (node-side port allocation is sp-npxq.3 — out of scope.)
- **Built-in shell runnable:** `shell)` case → transparent-tmux `bash`. ✓
- **Byte-identical session #0:** entrypoint passes `--acp-port 7000 --tmux-session spawn --keepalive`; acp/served still `exec`; same env. Only intended delta: opencode-tui now tmux-wrapped. ✓
- **Out of scope respected:** no proto (.1), no node launch/exec code (.3), no web (.4). ✓
- **Testability:** `sh -n` + optional shellcheck + arg-parse smoke (Task 2), image build + in-container launcher/terminfo/transparency smoke (Task 5); live e2e deferred to sp-npxq.5. ✓
```

Review fixes: spawn-tui fails loud if no opencode backend, whitespace/glob-safe tmux env forwarding, spec --keepalive documented.

title: Session model + registry + proto/RPC (CP<->node): N sessions per spawn
description: Foundational: model multiple sessions within one spawn. A 'session' = {id, kind: agent-acp|agent-tui|shell, runnable?, status, endpoint (acp port / tmux session name / exec pid)}. The existing primary agent becomes session #0. Add CP proto + RPCs: CreateSession(spawnId, kind, runnableId?), ListSessions(spawnId), CloseSession(sessionId); thread CP<->node (node owns the in-container lifecycle). Per-session idle/lifecycle + reap on close. No UI yet.
close_reason: Done on branch sp-npxq-tabs (commits f521889..3348d01 + review fix 7e8478d). (spawn_id,session_id) model: proto session_id on Frame/SessionOpen/SessionClose + SessionTransport/State enums, SessionInfo/Roster/Status, CreateSession/CloseSession threaded through Attach stream; cp.v1 ListSessions/Create/CloseSession served from CP roster mirror. Node per-spawn sessionRegistry, pumps/relays re-keyed by (spawn,session), primary auto-registered as pinned #0 (id 0), roster-before-Bind race handled. Launch/reap deferred to sp-npxq.3 (TODO markers). Spec+quality reviews pass; clone-on-read data-race + input validation + DropNode pending purge fixed.

title: (EPIC) [demo-mvp] Spawn-view tab bar: multiple sessions per spawn (agents + shell)
description:
Add a tab bar atop the web UI's spawn view so one spawn can host MULTIPLE concurrent sessions inside its ONE container (shared filesystem + sidecar). Tab 1 = the current primary surface (native web ChatView for acp/served, term.js TUI for tmux). A '+' button opens a new tab that is either: (a) a NEW AGENT SESSION of a user-chosen runnable (acp-mode -> its own native-web ChatView tab; tui-mode -> its own term.js/tmux tab), or (b) a term.js BASH SHELL into the spawn's container.

DECISIONS (owner, 2026-06-07): tabs map to multiple sessions WITHIN the single spawn container (NOT separate spawns) — additional sessions launched via docker exec into the running container, reusing the terminal-attach PTY path. MVP scope INCLUDES native-web ACP additional agents (each needs its own in-container ACP endpoint + a node Pump + a ChatView tab), plus term.js shell and tui-agent tabs.

This is a real backend feature, not just UI: today a spawn runs ONE primary agent (entrypoint dispatches one runnable). Multi-session-per-spawn needs: a per-spawn session registry + lifecycle (create/list/close), the node launching additional sessions into a running container (shell via docker exec; tui-agent via docker exec spawn-tmux <runnable> in a uniquely-named tmux session; acp-agent via a second acpmux/acp endpoint on a fresh port with its own node Pump), proto/RPC session CRUD threaded CP<->node, the web tab bar + per-tab surface wiring (multiple concurrent ChatView/TerminalView sockets bound to session ids), and reuse of the per-runnable launch/env setup (opencode sidecar config, goose env, claude apiKeyHelper, tmux wrapping — factor out of the entrypoint dispatcher so exec-launched sessions get the same tweaks). Coordinate with the URL-history work (sp-jpn) for per-tab deep links and the Marketplace->Templates rename (sp-u5q). Rough child tasks filed; impl plans per task.
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE on branch sp-npxq-tabs. Multiple sessions per spawn (agents + shell) with a spawn-view tab bar: (spawn_id,session_id) model + in-stream node-authoritative proto/CP-roster (.1); reusable launcher + transparent tmux for all mosh sessions incl opencode (.2); node N-pumps/relays launch/reap with acp port pool 32668-32767 (.3); web keep-alive tabs (DIY keep-all-mounted/CSS-hide, no lib) + Zustand per-session store + '+' menu/CloseSession (.4); live e2e green (.5). opencode-tui reconciled role-agnostic; CP router fixed to key clients by (sessionID,clientID). Per-tab URL deep-linking deferred to post-merge phase (extend landed wouter/Nav). All tasks two-stage reviewed (spec+quality); Go+web units green; live multi-session e2e passes.

title: ACP session_info_update -> agent-supplied conversation title
description: Deferred from the ACP enrichment spec (2026-06-07). Surface the agent's session_info_update (a human-readable title like 'Fix auth bug in login flow') through a Frame field and onto the session / document.title. LOW commonality (only opencode ✅ among current agents; goose ✖, Gemini ✖, Claude Code ◑) and UNSTABLE in ACP, so guard behind presence. Independent of the spawn name: sp-jpn sets document.title from the spawn/app name and does NOT need this; this only adds an optional agent-generated descriptive title on top. Cheap (one Frame field + setter). sp-jpn depends on this so the title work is done once against the richer source rather than twice.

title: ACP session/load + cross-restart history replay (decode/render enrichment follow-up)
description: Deferred from the ACP enrichment spec (2026-06-07). Our existing Frame Seq-log replay already reconstructs the LIVE session for late-joining/reconnecting clients (acpmux/pump + reset cursor), so in-session reconnect is solved. This item is the BIGGER lifecycle piece: use ACP session/load to reconstruct a session the node no longer holds in memory (cold start, agent/container restart) by asking the agent to re-stream persisted history, and reconcile that replayed history with our Frame log so we don't double-render. Support: opencode ✅, goose ◑ (issue #5916 historically off/partial), Claude Code ✅ (JSONL), Gemini ✅. Overlaps the shared-attach epic and sp-881 (local-app data sync / IDE ACP client). Not decode/render-side; pull in only when cross-restart durability is needed.

title: claude-acp: wrap the Claude Agent SDK in an ACP-stdio shim (native web + shared nori terminal)
description:
Add a 'claude-acp' runnable: a small ACP-over-stdio shim backed by the Anthropic Claude Agent SDK (programmatic agent SDK, formerly Claude Code SDK; TS @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk or Python). The shim speaks canonical ACP (initialize / session/new / session/prompt / session/update incl. tool_call CONTENT + session/request_permission, and ideally the richer variants — plans, diffs, etc.) and maps it to the SDK's streaming query + tool/permission callbacks. Analogous to ocadapter (opencode HTTP+SSE -> ACP) but for the Claude Agent SDK.

PAYOFF: gives Claude the SAME dual-surface goose-acp has, which today it lacks (Claude is tmux-only via claude-tui, no rich web): (1) NATIVE WEB ChatView — claude-acp -> node pump / acpmux -> web; (2) nori in the TERMINAL backed by Claude, sharing the SAME live conversation via acpdial -> acpmux. Reuses the sp-9xr.12 machinery (acpmux single-session multiplexer + nori acpdial shared attach) verbatim — claude-acp is just another ModeACP/RelayPump runnable.

COMPOSITION/DEPS: register a runnable in internal/agentcaps (e.g. claude-code -> claude-acp, ModeACP, RelayPump) + a dispatcher case (run the shim, multiplexed by acpmux for the shared session) + embed the SDK + its runtime in the agent image (NOTE: image currently has NO node/npm — claude-code is the native binary; a TS SDK shim needs a Node runtime, or use the Python SDK — decide at impl). For non-Anthropic models, the SDK can target the sidecar's /v1/messages Anthropic<->OpenAI converter (sp-9xr.15) via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, or use real Anthropic directly.

SEQUENCING (owner): do this AFTER the ACP enrichment (this epic's research-driven capability work) — once we know/decode the rich ACP capabilities (tool content/diffs, plans, todos, modes, fs/terminal), the shim can emit them and we get a high-fidelity Claude-on-ACP rather than a minimal-slice one. Depends on the enrichment children (to be filed from the research).

title: tmux/terminal-mode polish: opencode-tui sidecar config, status dot, claude key warning, UTF-8 locale
description:
Four bugs found running terminal-mode (tmux) spawns in the web UI + spawnctl attach:
(1) opencode-tui bypasses the sidecar: the dispatcher's opencode-tui case is 'exec spawn-tmux opencode' and SKIPS the opencode.json sidecar-provider config + OPENCODE_CONFIG + model=spawnery/<id> that the default opencode-served path generates, so the TUI uses opencode's built-in provider (zen) and autogenerates the session name. Fix: run the same config-gen (factor a shared shell fn) before launching the TUI so it goes through the sidecar.
(2) Web status dot wrong for tmux mode: openSession is gated off for tmux (App.tsx) and TerminalView's own socket has an empty onDown and never reports up, so ConnStatus.conn stays null -> dot hidden. Fix: TerminalView reports its socket state into useConnStatus (thread the callbacks down).
(3) Claude Code prints 'Detected a custom API key in your environment' because ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is in the env. Fix: provide the dummy key via an apiKeyHelper (settings.json) instead of the env var so no warning fires (verify live; fall back to customApiKeyResponses preseed if needed).
(4) Broken glyphs: tmux shows VT100 ACS line-drawing as q/x and claude ASCII-substitutes (●->_) in the web. Root cause: image has no UTF-8 locale (debian:stable, LANG unset=POSIX). Fix: ENV LANG=C.UTF-8 LC_ALL=C.UTF-8 (C.utf8 is available, no locales pkg needed); spawn-tmux 'tmux -u'; ExecPrefixFor forwards -e LANG/LC_ALL (both docker + crictl lanes). One root cause for both tmux q/x and web ●->_.
close_reason: 4 terminal-mode bugs fixed + verified live: (1) opencode-tui now runs the sidecar provider config (factored setup_opencode_provider() shared with opencode-served) so it no longer hits opencode zen; (2) web status dot driven by TerminalView reporting its socket state into useConnStatus (App->AppShell->TerminalView), incl. F1 guard against stray reconnecting() on intentional teardown; (3) claude 'custom API key in environment' warning removed by supplying the dummy key via apiKeyHelper (claude-settings.json) instead of ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env (verified no warning + TestCPClaudeTuiEndToEnd still passes via sidecar); (4) UTF-8 glyphs fixed (ENV LANG/LC_ALL=C.UTF-8 + tmux -u + ExecPrefixFor forwards LANG on docker lane; crictl relies on image ENV) — tmux/web render box+● not q/x/_. Reviewed APPROVED w/ minor; F1 fixed. e2e 7/7 PASS.

title: [demo-mvp] Web UI: URL history + document.title reflect the current view
description: Today the web SPA switches views via React useState only (web/src/App.tsx + shell/AppShell.tsx + Sidebar) — no router, no browser-history integration, and document.title is static. Result: Back/Forward don't work, views aren't deep-linkable/shareable/refresh-stable, and the tab title never reflects context. Make navigation update the URL + title as the user moves between app parts. Requirements: (1) push a history entry (history.pushState) for each navigable view — marketplace/templates list, app Detail (e.g. /app/<id>), spawns list, active spawn (e.g. /spawn/<id>), and the other top-level sections; (2) handle popstate so Back/Forward restore the corresponding view state (selected app/spawn, active tab); (3) on first load, parse the URL so a deep link / refresh lands on the right view; (4) set document.title per view (e.g. 'Spawnery — Templates', 'Spawnery — <app title>', 'Spawnery — <spawn name>'). Keep it lightweight — a tiny pushState/popstate helper is fine; a full router lib only if it pulls its weight. Don't break the live /ws/session handling (App.tsx uses location.host for the socket). Coordinate path/title naming with the Marketplace->Templates rename ([[sp-u5q]]) so URLs/titles use the final names. Update Playwright e2e (e2e/*.spec.ts) that assume in-place view switching to also assert URL + title where relevant.
close_reason: Epic complete: URL history + document.title via wouter; deep-link/refresh/back-forward; Marketplace->Templates rename (sp-u5q). 146 vitest green, e2e authored. Merged to master --no-ff. Children .1-.5 done.

description: Enrich ocadapter (opencode->ACP), the proxies (acpmux/pump), our ACP client (internal/acp), and the downstream Frame protocol/web ChatView to surface far more agent capabilities than today's minimal slice (we currently decode only: text, thoughts, tool title+status, turn state, permissions; everything richer — tool content/diffs, cost/tokens, model attribution, plans, commands, modes, fs/*, terminal/*, lifecycle — is dropped). DRIVEN BY a deep-research capability map: see docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-07-acp-capability-map-prompt.md. Implement in a separate session.

description: User direction: ONE agent image containing all agents (goose, claude-code, opencode, toad) + tmux; choose agent at runtime by overriding the entrypoint with the runnable's complete launch command. Requires: install all agent binaries in deploy/agent/Dockerfile; a complete per-runnable Launch (baked launch scripts incl. each agent's sidecar/model config + ACP adapter for acp/served, tmux wrap for tui); set AGENT_BINARIES to all of them; manager overrides entrypoint for ALL selected runnables (not just tmux). Supersedes sp-9xr.3.8 (the argv-aware-entrypoint mechanism lands in sp-9xr.5).

Remaining per-agent wiring tracked as follow-ups: claude converter sp-9xr.15, goose-acp bridge sp-9xr.16, nori ACP-TUI client sp-9xr.12. AGENT_BINARIES advertises wired agents per node config.

title: agent images must honor runnable launch argv (docker ENTRYPOINT vs Cmd)
description: sp-9xr.3.6 threads AgentSpec.Cmd. On the docker lane, container Config.Cmd becomes ARGS to the image ENTRYPOINT (deploy/agent uses tini -- entrypoint.sh), so a selected NON-default runnable (e.g. goose-acp) won't actually launch until agent images are made argv-aware (exec-form passthrough / no opinionated entrypoint) — and real goose/claude images exist. CRI maps Cmd to Command (overrides entrypoint), so the lanes also diverge. Today only the opencode/stub default path runs, so not a live bug. Verify with the Docker e2e when real multi-runnable images land.

title: Assess + decide: relax agent caps (CHOWN/SETUID/SETGID) so runtime apt works in spawns
description: The agent container runs CapDrop=ALL (manager.go DropAllCaps:true), so runtime 'apt update/install' inside a spawn fails: apt drops to the _apt user (setuid/setgid/setgroups) for its download sandbox and chowns its cache, all needing CAP_SETUID/CAP_SETGID/CAP_CHOWN. Today we bake tools into the image instead. TASK: assess the exact risk of granting back ONLY {CHOWN, SETUID, SETGID} to the agent and decide whether to enable it (likely dev-only opt-in env, prod stays CapDrop=ALL). Notes: (1) most defensible on the runsc/gVisor lane where gVisor sandboxes syscalls regardless of in-container caps; (2) no-cap workaround exists today: 'apt-get -o APT::Sandbox::User=root update' (update works; install postinst chowns may still fail); (3) risk surface: in-container setuid/chown could aid privilege games within the container, but container escape still needs a runtime/kernel/gVisor bug. Deliverable: risk write-up + decision (enable dev-only / keep hardened / sandbox=root-only). MVP-scope per owner.

title: CP: handle duplicate node-id registration (displace old stream + epoch-guard teardown)
description: registry.Add keys by node-id (r.m[id]=n), so a 2nd node registering the same id silently overwrites the registry entry while the 1st stream stays alive. Both streams' Heartbeat/reconcileInventory act on the shared id (the duplicate that doesn't host a spawn marks it UNREACHABLE), and EITHER stream's disconnect defer runs DropNode(id)+MarkUnreachable, killing routes for a spawn that's fine on the other. Symptom: spawn flips to UNREACHABLE shortly after create + web can't connect when two spawnlets share NODE_ID. FIX: on Register with an existing id, displace (close/cancel) the OLD connection and take over; guard the per-connection defer so it only Remove/DropNode when the registry entry is STILL this connection (epoch/token), so the displaced old's teardown doesn't nuke the new node's routes. Node-side fail-fast (terminal port bind) already added as a local guard for the single-host case.
close_reason: Implemented: registry per-connection token + lastBeat; Register rejects duplicate id when existing node is alive (heartbeat <15s), silently displaces a dead one; runNode teardown token-guarded (RemoveIfCurrent) + displaced streams stop (IsCurrent). Node-side fail-fast on terminal-port bind already added for the same-host case. Unit tests cover reject-alive/displace-dead/epoch-guard.

Only the UDP data plane bypasses CP.
notes: STANDALONE PATH DONE: spawnctl tmux talks DIRECTLY to the node's /terminal HTTP endpoint (per 'mosh around CP for now'); mosh UDP direct to node. REMAINING (CP-attached mode productionization): route the control step through CP — owner-only authz, node discovery, auto-resume-if-suspended, and a CP<->node control message carrying {host,port,key} (no buf available here for the proto add). The data plane stays direct.

Start a mosh-server whose child command is an exec-PTY into the spawn container (per lane: docker exec / CRI exec) running 'tmux new-session -A <name>' -> 'opencode attach http://127.0.0.1:4096'. Track the per-spawn tmux/mosh session for reattach; allocate/clean up UDP ports; return {UDP port, mosh key}. Idle: only opencode-observable activity counts (terminal keystrokes bypass the adapter, so a silent TUI can be idle-suspended) — accepted; no keepalive in this scope.
close_reason: Node terminal launcher done: StartTerminal runs mosh-server whose child execs into the spawn container (docker/crictl exec) running 'tmux new-session -A opencode attach <url> -s/-c' on the shared opencode session. ExecPrefixFor picks docker vs crictl by lane. Real mosh-server launch+parse validated (integration test); in-container tmux+opencode-attach renders the web's session (shared visibility) validated on real docker.

Update e2e/marketplace.spec.ts + e2e/spawn-lifecycle.spec.ts (which reference those testids) in lockstep. Minimum = user-visible labels; recommended = component/dir/testid identifiers too for consistency. Also sweep docs (MANUAL_VERIFICATION §B-G, README, deployment) for 'marketplace' copy.
acceptance_criteria: Nav + header read 'Templates'; no user-facing 'Marketplace' string remains; vitest + Playwright e2e updated and green; doc copy updated.

title: [demo-mvp] Demo-time observability view (live tail of telemetry + metrics)
description: A minimal way to WATCH the system during the demo: tail the telemetry JSONL into a readable live view + a one-screen metrics summary (scrape /metrics) showing active spawns, nodes up, and recent lifecycle/failure events. Goal: failures are visible on stage, not silent. Can be a just recipe + small script, not a full dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: One command shows live spawn lifecycle + node status during a demo run; a spawn error / node drop is visibly reflected within seconds.

title: Plan 2 pump-wiring interface requirements (from Plan-1 review)
description: Surfaced by the Plan-1 (sp-bjd) pump-core final review; must be handled when wiring the pump live: (1) pump.stop() must invoke runtime.AttachedStream.Close (full hijack+stdin teardown), NOT the current Stdout io.Closer cast (a hermetic stand-in) -- newPump should take the agent's close func. (2) Surface pump.readerDone / an onAgentExit callback so the integration detects agent death -> mark spawn ERROR + emit a final idle/error turn frame (else busy stays true -> stuck 'working...'). (3) Plumb goose's real permission title/tool from the request params into perm_request (currently hard-coded 'permission requested'). (4) Add a multi-client-detach-mid-IN-FLIGHT-turn test (needs a pausable fake agent). Part of the sp-bjd Plan 2 integration.
close_reason: Pump-wiring interface reqs done in Plan 2: stop() calls AttachedStream.Close via closeFn; exitFn surfaces agent-death (ERROR + reclaim pump/active/container). Permission title still generic + the multi-client-detach-mid-in-flight-turn test remain minor follow-ups (see new cleanup bead). Done with sp-bjd.

title: MVP: attachable knowledge llm-wikis — claim-level provenance + /wiki-consult
description:
First deliverable toward attachable knowledge llm-wikis: make a produced vault a trust-first store of provenanced facts an agent can consult.

Scope (punch-list items 1, 2, 3, 6):
- Claim-level knowledge: block-anchored body bullets (^c-xxx) + frontmatter claims: map (sources, by, asserted_at, confidence, superseded_by). Uniform across entity/concept/synthesis.
- Per-fact provenance (#1) and write-identity (#2, by ∈ {human|wiki-research|deep-research|import}).
- Claim-level in-page supersession (#3); old claims move to a ## Superseded section, qmd-queryable.
- Synthesis becomes a living page (write-once dropped; integrity now from claim-level supersession).
- New read-only /wiki-consult skill (#6): trust-ranked, provenance-annotated, freshness-aware (180d), contradiction-flagging, hybrid scope, no web, no writes; suggests /wiki-research on gaps.
- wiki-research retrieval (Phase 2-3) refactored to delegate to wiki-consult's shared logic; Phase 7 emits claims.

All changes are edits to llm-wiki's RUNBOOK.md (the artifact). Forward-only migration; old free-text bullets stay valid as un-provenanced notes.

Deferred follow-ups (NOT in this MVP): #4 consult-reflex/auto-trigger; #5 cross-repo attachment (consult vault Z from project A's session).
design: Full spec lives in the llm-wiki repo at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-claim-level-knowledge-and-wiki-consult-design.md (committed f05f390). Execute in a separate llm-wiki session via writing-plans. RUNBOOK sections touched: II.1-II.2, II.6 (rewrite), II.8, Phase 2 templates, Phase 3 skills, Phase 5 lint.
notes: Spec authored in a spawnery brainstorming session 2026-06-03; work executes in the separate llm-wiki repo (/home/debian/AleCode/llm-wiki), which has no own beads tracker, so the issue is tracked here in spawnery per user.

title: Research GPT Store (and peer stores) to verify Spawnery differentiation hypotheses
description:
Competitive/positioning research feeding the eval-harness design (sp-dkz §7) and demo messaging. Verify (confirm/refute, with sources) four hypotheses about why GPT Store apps are structurally weaker than Spawnery Apps:

H1 — NO take-home artifacts: GPT Store apps cannot produce a durable, user-owned, portable artifact store (no per-user data repo the app accumulates + user can export/clone). => LLM-wiki-style 'owned growing corpus' apps impossible. CAVEAT to check: Code Interpreter can emit one-off downloadable files in-session; confirm there is no PERSISTENT user-owned repo across sessions.

H2 — NO user/creator containers: apps = prompt + creator knowledge files + Actions (calls to an EXTERNAL API the creator hosts elsewhere) + OpenAI's own ephemeral Code Interpreter sandbox (runs the MODEL's generated python, not creator-provided container code). => zork-game-master / persistent-server-state apps not expressible as creator-controlled containerized code. CAVEAT: an Action could call the creator's own externally-hosted server — note this is 'you host it elsewhere', not the store running your container/OCI image.

H3 — bespoke UI creation: GPT Store GPTs are authored via the GPT Builder UI (conversational builder + configure form), NOT as a git repo you build with your own agent-coding tooling (Claude Code etc.) and publish. CAVEAT: OpenAI Assistants API is programmatic but is a SEPARATE product from the consumer GPT Store publish path — confirm you cannot push a repo-built artifact to the Store.

H4 — no evals in creator studio: the GPT Builder has only a manual live 'preview' chat; no automated scenario testing / simulated-user / scoring. Confirm.

For each: cite sources (current as of 2026) and map to Spawnery's contrasting capability (data repo / hardened container + ACP / repo-built apps / eval harness sp-dkz). Output feeds spec §7 positioning + demo-MVP §5 messaging rules. Peer-store spot-check (Poe/Coze/Character.AI) where cheap.

title: Eval harness MVP — creator-private 'Test Your App' (measurement-only slice of sp-kkk)
description:
MVP 'taste of orchestration': pull the MEASUREMENT-ONLY slice of sp-kkk forward. A first-party, operator-run harness drives an App-under-test (SUBJECT spawn) through a creator-authored scenario via a DRIVER/mock-human (ACP persona) and grades the resulting ACP transcript with a JUDGE (E8 scanner sp-ydz reused in rubric mode). Creator-private 'Test Your App' report (transcript + per-dimension scorecard). Three modes: Scenario, Replay (grade a recorded conversation, no mock-human), Diff (re-run scenarios across app@sha).

EXPLICIT NON-GOALS (MVP): no optimizer (-> sp-kkk phase 2); no native spawn-to-spawn comms / app-spawns-app (-> sp-0tw); no public quality badge / eval-gated trust tier (-> long-term arc, gated on BYOM for cross-family judging); no user-facing multi-agent.

KEY: orchestrator is operator-run (spawnctl-style over internal/acp), NOT a marketplace app, so it does NOT need sp-0tw and sidesteps the spawn-creation security crux. Depends only on the existing demo spawn engine.

Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-eval-harness-mvp-design.md. Backed by two deep-research passes (2026-06-03) + competitive verification (AgentCore/Braintrust/LangSmith). Per planning workflow: design only, no impl until all epic designs done + adversarial roast.

title: WS auto-reconnect via partysocket + escalating connect-timeout + reconnecting indicator
description: Replace the chat WebSocket's single new WebSocket() with a partysocket-backed self-reconnecting socket (web/src/shell/reconnectingSocket.ts) that retries forever with an escalating per-attempt connect-timeout (500/2.5s/5s/15s/30s, success resets to 500; via mutating partysocket _options.connectionTimeout on close). Re-run the ACP handshake on each (re)connect; teardown closes the socket; ledger poll still owns connect/teardown while partysocket owns WS-level reconnect. useConnStatus gains reconnecting (yellow) + disconnected (red after ~12s grace). Pin partysocket exact version + structural guard test. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-ws-reconnect-partysocket-design.md.
close_reason: WS auto-reconnect shipped & merged: ReconnectingSocket (partysocket@1.1.19 pinned) with escalating connect-timeout 500ms/2.5s/5s/15s/30s, handshake re-runs per (re)connect, useConnStatus reconnecting(yellow)/disconnected(red after grace), guard+behavior tests. (Re-closing: today's branch checkout/merge reverted the original close commit 4f1de17 via the .beads import hook.)

title: Background-spawn 'working' indicator in sidebar (per-spawn turn-state beyond the live WS)
description:
Deferred from the chat turn-state/queueing work (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-chat-turn-state-and-queueing-design.md). That MVP shows the 'working…' affordance only for the ACTIVE spawn, because turn-state is delivered over the single live WS (spawn/turn notification + spawn/history.turn). To show which BACKGROUND spawns are mid-turn (sidebar dots), turn-state must propagate beyond the one live connection.

Approaches to weigh (discuss before building):
- Fast-track 'busy' status propagation over WS.
- Retain WS connections when switching between spawns (at least for a while, with a timeout) so background turn-state keeps flowing.
- Full plumb-through: turn-state up through CP + DB (relates to sp-pc4 CP state/DAO) so listSpawns/ledger carries a per-spawn busy bit the sidebar polls.

Likely interacts with sp-r7t (node-owned session / reconnect) and sp-pc4 (CP state layer).

title: Node readiness probe: report ACTIVE only after the agent answers ACP initialize
description: Today the node reports ACTIVE the instant mgr.Create returns (containers launched), but goose takes ~5s more to answer ACP initialize — so the spawn shows green ~instantly while the WS can't connect for ~5s, defeating the sp-wi3 starting lifecycle. Fix: in attacher.startSpawn, after Create, open an attach and send an ACP initialize, waiting for the matching response (readiness probe) before reporting ACTIVE; timeout(30s)->mgr.Stop+ERROR. Lane-agnostic via mgr.Attach (Docker stdio / CRI adapter UDS), retrying the attach while it fails (CRI adapter startup). Repeated initialize verified safe vs goose (see sp-r7t, the spec-compliant follow-on). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-node-readiness-probe-design.md.
close_reason: Node readiness probe: startSpawn now sends an ACP initialize and awaits the response (probeReady, retry-attach to 30s for CRI adapter startup) before reporting ACTIVE; timeout->mgr.Stop+ERROR. So the UI's green dot means 'agent answered' not just 'container launched', and yellow/starting covers the ~5s goose boot. awaitInitialize unit-tested (6 cases); Manager.Stop made teardown cancellation-immune. 2 tasks two-stage reviewed; final whole-branch review READY-TO-MERGE; e2e 11/11 clean (probe runs in the real node lane vs stub). Follow-ups: sp-r14 (probeReady retry-loop test). Merged to master.

title: Visible spawn 'starting' lifecycle: async CreateSpawn + yellow dot, WS gated on active, 'waiting' header state
description: Today CP CreateSpawn blocks until the spawn is ACTIVE, so the web never shows a 'starting' period. Make CreateSpawn async: validate sync, store.Create(starting), return id immediately, provision in a background goroutine that SetActive/SetError on the node signal (status already DB-recorded). Web: createSpawn returns starting -> add to list (yellow dot) + setActiveId + conn=waiting, do NOT openSession; poll ListSpawns (1s while any starting, else 3s) and openSession when the active spawn flips to active (green), errored() on error (red, stays in sidebar, removable via Stop). useConnStatus/ConnStatus gain a 'waiting' state (grey pulse). Sidebar starting dot = yellow. Chat input disabled until connected. CP tests: createActive + suspend/resume tests wait-for-active after async CreateSpawn (new waitActive helper). spawnctl uses standalone spawn.v1, unaffected. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-02-spawn-starting-lifecycle-design.md.
close_reason: Visible spawn starting lifecycle. CP CreateSpawn async (validate sync, store.Create starting, return id, background provisionSpawn -> SetActive/SetError on node signal, logs provision failures). Web: yellow starting dot, grey-pulse 'waiting' header, WS gated on active via poll (1s while starting else 3s) + pure nextConnAction policy, failed/unreachable spawns red+removable, chat input gated on connected. 3 tasks two-stage reviewed; final review READY-TO-MERGE; e2e 10/10 (retries:2 absorbs ERR_NETWORK_CHANGED env flake). Merged to master.

title: Node-relay transcript recording + replay (history survives browser reload, both lanes)
description: The slice-2 spawn/history replay lives in the in-pod acpadapter, which is only in the CRI/runsc lane — so in the Docker/self-hosted lane (just dev) history is lost on browser reload (client buffer gone, no adapter in path). Move recording to the NODE relay (in both lanes path, outlives client reloads): extract the recorder to internal/transcript (shared with the adapter); node holds a long-lived per-spawn recorder registry, tees relay bytes, and replays spawn/history on each client (re)connect. Lane-aware (node records Docker lane only; CRI keeps the adapter) to avoid double-replay. Web onHistory/historyToItems unchanged. Goal: history survives reload in just dev, proven by an e2e test. Follows-on from sp-r6b/sp-02f; partial step toward sp-3nb (durable/cross-device history).
close_reason: Node-relay transcript recording + replay. Recorder extracted to internal/transcript (shared w/ adapter); node records each spawn's transcript at the relay + replays spawn/history on every client (re)connect, Docker lane only (lane-aware). History now survives a browser reload in just dev — proven by e2e (10/10, reload test wipes the client buffer so only node replay can restore). 3 tasks two-stage reviewed; final review READY-TO-MERGE.

title: Web: remove static Chat tab + auto-spawn; spawn from Marketplace, active spawn under Spawns sidebar; seed secret-app in catalog
description: The left-sidebar Chat tab is a hardcoded mount-time auto-spawn of APP_ID='secret-app' (App.tsx useEffect), but the catalog only seeds 4 themed clones (wiki/language/interview/zork, all Ref=examples/secret-app) — no app id 'secret-app' exists, so the auto-spawn's createSpawn('secret-app') fails. Fix (user-approved #3): (web) drop the mount-time auto-spawn; remove the Chat nav item; default view=Marketplace; surface the active spawn under the Sidebar 'Spawns' section (currently dead '— none yet —') as a clickable item that opens its chat (chat's new home, gives Marketplace<->spawn back-nav); update AppShell/Sidebar tests. (CP seed) add a 'secret-app' AppSeed (Ref=examples/secret-app, public+listed+reviewed, mount main) so it's browsable + spawnable for e2e smoke on staging.
close_reason: DONE merged 2768e0d. (web) removed mount-time auto-spawn + APP_ID; Sidebar drops the Chat nav (market+settings only), default view=market; active spawn surfaces under the 'Spawns' section as nav-spawn -> opens chat (App.activeApp threaded App->AppShell->Sidebar); initial status='' busy=false. (cp seed) added spawnery/secret-app (DisplayName 'Secret App', Ref=examples/secret-app, public+listed+reviewed) browsable+spawnable. 27 vitest pass, tsc clean, go suite green. Follow-up sp-aqv (host-gated Playwright chat.spec.ts still references nav-chat).

title: Node should retry/reconnect to CP (not exit when CP is down)
description: Observed during just dev: if CP isn't up yet at node startup (or the CP drops), node.Run returns an error and cmd/spawnlet log.Fatals -> the node exits immediately. Fix: a reconnect loop with backoff so the node waits for the CP at startup and reconnects if the stream drops (re-Register; the CP already reconciles a returning node per the state-machine design). Only exit on ctx cancellation (clean shutdown). The Manager + its running spawns persist across reconnects (mgr is passed into node.Run; a fresh attacher re-registers).
close_reason: DONE merged a8343cd. node.Run refactored into a reconnect loop + per-connection runOnce: dials+Registers+heartbeats+receives under a per-connection connCtx (defer cancel stops heartbeat + relay sessions on disconnect, no goroutine leak); backoff 1s->30s exp (reset after >30s healthy); returns ONLY on ctx cancellation. cmd/spawnlet log.Fatal(node.Run) with ctx=Background now loops forever (reconnects) instead of dying when CP is down. Fixes the just-dev startup race + CP-restart resilience. mgr persists across reconnects (running spawns survive). Hermetic TestRunRetriesUntilContextCancelled (unreachable CP -> returns context err after retrying, not the conn err). First internal/node test (partial sp-sj8).

title: runsc-pod slice 4: CRI egress floor (SPAWNLET-EGRESS) + host ops
description: CNIFloorApplier: spawnlet-owned SPAWNLET-EGRESS chain in filter, -I FORWARD 1 -j (before CNI-FORWARD), per-pod -s podIP rules, boot+periodic reconcile of the jump. Same firewall.Rules content as DOCKER-USER; Docker path left untouched. containerd runsc-handler + CNI bridge/firewall/portmap conflist config; image-store bridge docs in deployment.md/ISOLATION.md. Host-gated egress_e2e extension. Spec sec3.4, slice 4.
close_reason: DONE merged 143ab43 (slice 4 SPAWNLET-EGRESS floor).

title: runsc-pod slice 5: wire-up + end-to-end runsc spawn
description: Select backend + floor applier by CONTAINER_RUNTIME in cmd/spawnlet; CRI preflight wired. Run the full goose+sidecar spawn under runsc (the round-trip sp-vaw blocked on): agent reaches sidecar on 127.0.0.1:8080, ACP round-trip, clean teardown (no leaked netns/images over N cycles); host-verify the SPAWNLET-EGRESS floor under the pod model. Closes sp-vaw. Spec sec5, slice 5.
notes: DONE + verified on a real runsc host. CRI pod backend (internal/runtime/cri) wires RunPodSandbox(handler=runsc)+2 containers, image pull into k8s.io, ACP attach (now TCP on pod IP — see sp-vaw), SPAWNLET-EGRESS floor on the pod IP, RemovePodSandbox teardown, runsc preflight. End-to-end goose spawn via standalone node AND CP-attached node (cloud class) both returned 'QUOKKA-4417'; 5/5 clean cycles. Provisioned: gVisor runsc+shim, crictl, CNI v1.9.1, containerd runsc handler. Commit b4e1b4b.

title: runsc-pod slice 2: PodBackend seam + Docker backend
description: Extract a two-phase runtime.PodBackend interface (StartPod -> floor -> StartAgent -> Stop) both backends implement; reimplement the Docker/runc path behind it (docker_pod.go) over existing docker.go primitives. Manager holds a PodBackend (not a bare ContainerRuntime); Create becomes backend-agnostic with the no-unfirewalled-agent-window guarantee. Pure refactor, existing tests stay green. Spec sec3.1/sec3.2, slice 2.
notes: UNBLOCKED: sp-7wa (slice 1, UDS transport) merged 8207268. Next: extract the two-phase runtime.PodBackend seam + Docker backend behind it. AttachACP + Spawn.NetnsPath already exist; manager.Create already records netnsPath + attaches via AttachACP across all 3 relays.
close_reason: DONE merged. Two-phase runtime.PodBackend seam (Ping/Preflight/StartPod/StartAgent/Stop) + PodSpec/AgentSpec/Resources/PodHandle in internal/runtime/pod.go; DockerPodBackend (internal/runtime/docker_pod.go) wraps ContainerRuntime (sidecar owns netns, agent joins via NetnsOf, StartPod derives PodIP+NetnsPath). Manager drives StartPod->floor->StartAgent->Stop via m.pod; NewManager(rt,cfg) unchanged (wraps in NewDockerPodBackend); Runtime() removed. Pure refactor, all 14 spawnlet tests green unmodified. Follow-up sp-mcb (tolerate empty pod IP non-enforced).

title: runsc-pod slice 1: UDS ACP transport (both backends)
description: Replace stdio Attach with an in-pod Unix-domain-socket ACP bridge. Agent image gets an entrypoint adapter listening on abstract socket @spawnlet-acp that execs 'goose acp' and wires the socket<->stdio. Node gains runtime.AttachACP(netnsPath): setns into the pod netns (lock OS thread, switch, dial, restore), return AttachedStream. Switch the Docker/runc path to it (replace rt.Attach in ws.go/server.go). Keystone: de-risks the transport on the working runc path before any CRI code. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-runsc-cri-pod-backend-design.md sec3.3, slice 1.
design: UDS chosen over CRI Attach (research: CRI streaming flaky for multi-hour JSON-RPC). Abstract socket scoped to netns. setns thread-netns hazard: lock/switch/dial/restore.
close_reason: DONE merged 8207268. UDS ACP transport shipped: in-container acpadapter (@spawnlet-acp <-> goose acp stdio) + runtime.AttachACP (setns into pod netns + dial) + ALL THREE relay handlers switched (server.go Session, ws.go HandleWS, node/attach.go openSession) + goose/stubagent images + docs. Hermetic tests green; acp_e2e/e2e host-gated.

title: runsc-pod slice 3: CRI backend lifecycle
description: internal/runtime/cri: k8s.io/cri-api gRPC client to the containerd sock. RunPodSandbox(handler=runsc) + CreateContainer/StartContainer x2 (sidecar+agent) + StopPodSandbox/RemovePodSandbox. PodIP from PodSandboxStatus.Network.IP; NetnsPath from sandbox shim pid. Image pull via CRI ImageService into the k8s.io namespace. CRI preflight (canary pod). Gated behind CONTAINER_RUNTIME=runsc. Hermetic tests vs a fake CRI gRPC server. Spec sec3.2/sec3.5/sec3.6, slice 3.
close_reason: DONE merged 1931068 (slice 3 CRI backend). Re-closed after a .beads checkout reverted it.

title: spawnlet: runsc/runtime startup preflight — fail hard at boot if CONTAINER_RUNTIME can't run a smoke container
description: When CONTAINER_RUNTIME is set (e.g. runsc), spawnlet must validate it AT STARTUP (not at first CreateSpawn): runtime.RuntimeCheck / Manager.PreflightRuntime starts a tiny smoke container (agent image + Cmd ['true']) under the configured runtime, then stops it; any error -> log.Fatal (process exits). Catches missing/broken runsc early. Default (empty=runc) skips. Wire in cmd/spawnlet CP-attached startup.
close_reason: Done, merged b9f5342: Manager.PreflightRuntime runs a smoke ('true') container under CONTAINER_RUNTIME at spawnlet startup; cmd/spawnlet log.Fatal on failure. Empty runtime = no-op. Host-verified: 'true' under runsc OK.

title: [epic] Adopt gVisor (runsc) for the cloud tier — Phase 2 isolation
description: Research verdict (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-gvisor-isolation-research-results.md): adopt gVisor on the cloud tier now, on top of the existing floor. Same isolation as Cloud Run/GKE Sandbox/Modal/Tencent for ~identical workload; closes the host-kernel + runc-escape CVE class (CVE-2019-5736, CVE-2024-21626, CVE-2020-14386, CVE-2022-0185) that runc+seccomp does not. Plumbing exists (CONTAINER_RUNTIME=runsc knob). Plan: PHASE 1 (pre-req, cheap) read-only rootfs + drop-all caps + user-namespace remap + MOVE egress floor to host veth (see floor-placement bug). PHASE 2 install runsc (systrap platform, directfs+overlay+netstack defaults) + canary cloud spawns 5%->25%->100% + per-image runc fallback w/ audit + CI compat gate. cgroup limits (sp-ach) STAY (gVisor doesn't self-enforce in-sandbox limits — pin sandbox to host cgroup). PHASE 3 (threshold-gated) microVM (Kata/Cloud Hypervisor) for a hard tier: regulated/enterprise/high-value tenants, GPU/eBPF/FUSE/io_uring needs, or /dev/kvm available. Run our own benchmark vs real agent traffic before sizing.
notes: BLOCKER found in local goose+sidecar runsc verification (sp-vaw, P0): the current Docker two-container shared-netns pod (agent --network=container:<sidecar>, reaches sidecar on 127.0.0.1:8080) is BROKEN under per-container gVisor — each container is its own Sentry/netstack, loopback not shared. Verified: goose ITSELF is gVisor-compatible (boots+ACP+prompt under runsc); host egress floor (sp-ff2), cap-drop (sp-s9u), and runsc preflight (sp-zb2) all work under runsc on the real pod. The ONLY break is inter-container loopback. So Phase-2 needs a pod-as-one-sandbox model (containerd CRI pod / k8s RuntimeClass) OR collapsing sidecar+agent into one container — NOT raw docker-run-per-container. Decide in sp-vaw before any runsc cloud rollout.

title: Pre-gVisor hardening: read-only rootfs + drop-all caps + user-namespace remap on cloud spawns
description: Phase-1 hardening (gvisor.md §4 defense-in-depth), valuable under runc TODAY and a pre-req for gVisor: agent container ro rootfs + tmpfs scratch; --cap-drop=ALL (agent shell work needs none); user-namespace remap so in-sandbox root maps to an unprivileged host UID. Wire into spawnlet manager ContainerSpec/HostConfig. Cheap, big blast-radius reduction even before runsc.
close_reason: Done, merged b9f5342: agent container --cap-drop=ALL (always) + gated read-only rootfs (HARDEN_ROOTFS, default off) with /tmp tmpfs. userns-remap = daemon-config doc (deployment.md/ISOLATION.md), not per-container code. Host-verified under runsc: cap-drop + ro-rootfs (blocks rootfs writes, /tmp writable). ro-rootfs default-off pending goose image validation.

title: E6 marketplace web UI: Browse/Detail/Spawn + My Apps + Publish
description: React/web (web/) marketplace UI consuming the E5 catalog RPCs. 4-tab MarketplaceView (nested state, no router): Browse (ListApps+search+tier badges), Detail (GetApp manifest+versions+Spawn), My Apps (ListMyApps+takedown/relist SetAppListing), Publish (RegisterAppVersion form). API: extract shared unary->api/connect.ts + new api/catalog.ts (typed fns + tierLabel for Connect-JSON enum names). Spawn integration: refactor App.tsx to extract spawnApp(appId,model) re-spawn (teardown prior), thread onSpawn via AppShell+setView('chat'). Hand-rolled fetch, shadcn, data-testid, dev-token auth (existing patterns). Tests: Vitest+@testing-library hermetic (api+components); Playwright e2e host-gated. Spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-e6-marketplace-web-ui.md.
notes: VERIFIED + DONE (2026-06-04, Phase 5 on a real host). The full E6 marketplace web UI was exercised end-to-end via Playwright (web e2e 13/13): Browse (tier-badged cards + search), Detail (manifest + versions + Spawn), My Apps (takedown/relist toggle), Publish (form -> RegisterAppVersion). The 'Spawn re-entrancy guard' part of this scope had a real concurrency bug — spawning a second app while one was running delivered the prompt to the OLD spawn (stale 'connected' socket during the createSpawn await); fixed in commit 7ab6597 (App.tsx: enter pending state synchronously before createSpawn). MANUAL_VERIFICATION.md §G ticked.

title: Scheduler routing by node class + author-self-host rule for unverified apps
description: Use the CP-recorded node class (sp-2as) to place spawns. FLIPPED POLICY (user 2026-06-01): an UNVERIFIED app may run ONLY on a self-hosted node OWNED BY THE APP'S AUTHOR (creator_id == node owner). Rationale: unblocks the author's dev iteration with NO review and NO added risk (their own self-hosted node already has access to all the author's data; nothing new is exposed). scanned/reviewed apps route normally (cloud or any). Needs: node->owner association (node registers its owner; today node class is recorded but not node owner) + scheduler placement check tying app tier + creator_id to candidate node class+owner. Pure-CP once node-owner is propagated. Builds on sp-2as (node class) + sp-ach (floor).
close_reason: Done, merged: routing-by-node-class + author-self-host rule. node_owner propagated (Register->registry.Node.Owner, NODE_OWNER env); registry.Placement{Class,Owner}+PickFor; scheduler.Provision(placement); CreateSpawn computes placement from resolved tier — unverified requires caller==creator + self-hosted node owned by them (else PermissionDenied/ResourceExhausted, checked before minting), reviewed/scanned anywhere, empty version still LatestReviewed. Hermetic tests (PickFor, owner propagation, 3 policy outcomes) green; -race clean. Final review APPROVE.

title: CP: CP_STORE_DRIVER env to select sqlite/postgres (store already supports pg)

title: sp-pc4 part 3: persist container node_id + compensating cleanup on post-Provision failure
description: From the part-2 CP-rewire review. Three related robustness gaps deferred to part 3 (the inventory/reconciliation protocol): (1) CreateSpawn orphan window — if SetActive fails AFTER Provision succeeded (route bound + node container live), CreateSpawn returns CodeInternal with NO cleanup; the container + route leak and the row is stuck 'starting' (boot sweep flips it unreachable but the container/route survive). Add compensating rt.Drop + StopOnNode + SetError on the post-Provision failure path. (2) SetError's error is swallowed (_ =) on Provision failure — at least log it so a stuck 'starting' row isn't silent. (3) The container's node_id is NEVER persisted: Create inserts NodeID='' and SetActive only flips phase; Provision returns the chosen node id but CreateSpawn discards it. So spawn_containers.node_id stays empty and LiveContainersByNode/Adopt (the part-3 reconcile machinery) cannot function until the CP persists the node binding (e.g. SetActive should also set the container's node_id, or add a bind step). All three are the same theme: record the node binding + run compensating cleanup on the post-Provision edge. Part of sp-pc4 part 3 (lifecycle RPCs + generation fencing + inventory reconciliation).
close_reason: Done in sp-pc4 part 3a (merged 0978c24): node_id persisted via SetActive + compensating cleanup on the post-Provision orphan window. (Was filed depending-on sp-pc4, but it's a completed sub-item; sp-pc4 stays open for part 3b.)

title: CP state/DAO layer + full CP-side spawn lifecycle
description: Replace the CP's ephemeral in-memory maps with a durable transactional state layer (Bun + modernc sqlite + Postgres + goose + :memory: tests), and implement the ENTIRE CP-side spawn lifecycle on it. spawns table = the CP index. Owns: store package (schema owners/apps/app_versions/spawns/spawn_mounts, full interface, Bun impl, hermetic tests); CP-side state machine (starting/active/suspending/suspended/error/deleted) incl. transitions, boot + node-evict reconciliation (->suspended), idempotent seeding; lifecycle RPCs CreateSpawn(rewired)/ListSpawns/SuspendSpawn/ResumeSpawn/DeleteSpawn + Session ownership-via-store + auto-resume-on-attach + single-session takeover; CP->node plumbing (new Suspend message; StartSpawn carries mount bindings). Node-side persist/restore is a STUB teardown here (data survives only once E3 persistent storage + sp-gd9 WIP-ref land). DB authoritative for ownership; router.Owner() removed. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-state-dao-layer-design.md.
design: Hard constraints: ergonomic cross-repo transactions; thin-ORM scanning (no manual row->struct); SQL stays visible (no query-hiding ORM magic); one codebase over two dialects; strong (not absolute) pref for pure-Go/cgo-free; mature/boring, no bleeding edge. SQLite/Postgres are SEED options only — research must challenge the engine choice.
notes:
PARTS 1+2+3a DONE. Part1=store package (7672c95). Part2=CP rewire onto store (24f28ed). Part3a=ListSpawns+DeleteSpawn+node_id+compensating-cleanup (0978c24; closed sp-s5e). REMAINING = part 3b, gated on node (sp-gd9) + storage (E3): SuspendSpawn/ResumeSpawn/RecreateSpawn (still Unimplemented on the embedded handler), generation fencing of stale SpawnStatus, node inventory + grace-window + adopt reconciliation, Suspend node-message plumbing. Follow-up nit: see the DeleteSpawn/StopSpawn auth-ok consistency task.
PROGRESS (2026-06-04): RecreateSpawn implemented (was Unimplemented) + CP-side node-inventory reconciliation (reconcileInventory on Register/Heartbeat consumes the RunningSpawn report from sp-8hf -> marks active-but-unreported spawns unreachable). SuspendSpawn/ResumeSpawn already done (stub/non-lossless). Tests: TestRecreateSpawn, TestReconcileInventory; CP stub e2e green. REMAINING part-3b: lossless suspend via the Suspend CPMessage + per-mount WIP-ref persist/restore (HARD DEP E3 persistent storage), generation fencing on Stop (node ignores stale-gen), Suspend node-message plumbing.
