title: Orphaned-container handling: labels + reap-on-startup + idle reaper
description: The spawnlet's SpawnStore is in-memory, so on spawnlet restart it forgets running pods while Docker keeps them alive -> orphans. Also no idle-timeout in the slice, so clients that never call StopSpawn leak pods. DEFERRED from the slice (user decision: track only). Plan when picked up: (1) label every container at create (spawnery.managed=true, spawnery.spawn-id=<id>); (2) reconcile-on-startup: list labeled containers and remove orphans (clean slate; in-mem store is empty post-restart); (3) background IDLE-REAPER goroutine that tears down spawns idle past a timeout -- this is the E1 idle-timeout feature and is the only part needing an always-running process. Pairs with E1 runtime core (sp-ei4). For now: manual StopSpawn + docker rm for stragglers during dev.
notes: PROGRESS (2026-06-05): items 2+3+4 of the REMAINING list landed (TDD; node+spawnlet suites green, golangci-lint clean). Item 1 (CP inventory consumption) was already done via sp-pc4 reconcileInventory. (2) GENERATION FENCING on Stop: node now ignores a Stop whose generation is older than the live spawn (attach.go staleGen + Manager.SpawnGeneration); a current/0 gen tears down as before. Suspend half deferred -> the fence helper is ready but Suspend message handling itself is sp-gd9 (still an inert TODO in handle's default case). (3) IDLE-REAPER: Pump tracks lastActivity (refreshed by any relay frame in either direction) + attached(); attacher.reapIdle two-stage budgets (detached idleDetachedTimeout=15m, attached idleAttachedTimeout=60m) reaps via stopSpawn; idleReapLoop wired into runOnce (ticks every 1m, tied to connCtx). LOSSY teardown (reports STOPPED) — lossless suspend-on-idle is sp-gd9, gated on E3. (4) GRACEFUL SIGTERM TEARDOWN: Manager.StopAll(ctx); cmd/spawnlet uses signal.NotifyContext + gracefulStopAll after node.Run returns (fixes the sp-8mn e2e container leak). New tests: TestStopAllTearsDownEverySpawn, TestStopFencesStaleGeneration, TestPumpTracksActivityAndAttached, TestReapIdleTwoStage. NOT YET COMMITTED. All 3 title items (labels + reap-on-startup + idle reaper) now done -> issue is closeable, handing the Suspend-message fence + lossless idle-suspend to sp-gd9.
close_reason: Orphan-container handling complete: labels + reap-on-startup (prior, 57c7aba) + idle reaper (this session). Items 2/3/4 of the remaining list landed TDD: generation fencing on Stop (node ignores stale-gen), two-stage idle reaper (15m detached/60m attached, lossy STOPPED), graceful SIGTERM teardown (Manager.StopAll wired via signal.NotifyContext; fixes sp-8mn e2e leak). Item 1 was already done via sp-pc4 reconcileInventory. Suite green, golangci-lint clean. Handed to sp-gd9: lossless suspend-on-idle (E3-gated) + the Suspend-message fence (helper ready).

title: Proactive agent capability (scheduler + headless wake + notifications)
description: DEFERRED from the demo (E11.3). The agent runs + reaches the user when they didn't open the app: per-spawn SCHEDULER (decide it's time) + HEADLESS WAKE (run with no user watching) + NOTIFICATION channel (email/push). It is simultaneously several apps' reason-to-be (CRM follow-ups, wiki proactive cross-linking, language spaced-repetition) AND the portfolio's retention ENGINE (a proactive nudge = a return-loop driver; direct antidote to sp-54f). Cuts against the pure ephemeral/user-initiated model (new runtime capability). NAMED #1 post-demo retention lever. Unlocks: Personal CRM as a standalone app (currently folded into wiki). See E11 §3.

title: DEMO-ROAST: restrict agent toolset + audit-store liability + drop coaches
description: Three cheap demo de-riskers: (a) RESTRICT the agent's toolset for launch apps to file/search over /data (NO arbitrary shell/network fetch) -- the wiki doesn't need a shell, and the shell is the abuse vector; matches the original 'vetted tools' intent and shrinks the attack surface enormously. (b) AUDIT-STORE LIABILITY: do NOT persist severe-category flagged content (CSAM etc.) to the 30d audit store -- log the verdict + block, drop the bytes; storing it creates the liability it was meant to reduce. (c) SHIP WIKI-ONLY (+ zork as a toy), DROP both coaches from the demo -- weak-local-model-sensitive, uncanny, sp-pgw-unvalidated; fewer apps = less surface + best foot forward. Affects E7, E8 §1, demo-mvp-scope.

title: ROAST: ops hardening grab-bag
description: Found by ops #8/#9/#12/#13, product F6/F11. (a) Inline classifier runs per-turn on the SAME scarce home GPU (could ~2x load) — move to cheap CPU/small model, async fast-path for non-severe. (b) Burst gap/thundering herd: provisioning from zero takes 30s-min while home is already saturated — keep 1 pre-warmed standby, admission control + 'warming up' state, scale on leading indicator. (c) Audit store growth: full-content per turn — size/cost it, alert on stuck purge, KMS-managed key, tamper-evident access log. (d) Ad-hoc URL spawn: rate-limit per user, github.com-only, confirm scanner gates before exec (SSRF-ish). (e) Token refresh storms: proactive refresh + jittered expiry + coalesce. (f) Model contract may filter the free DeepSeek OUT of coaches needing big context (F6) — verify all 4 launch apps run acceptably on free DeepSeek or it's a bait-and-switch. Affects E8, E1 §5, E5 §3, E3 §3, E2.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: protocol/contract drift across E0/E1/E3/E4
description: Found by arch #8/#10/#11, security #6/#8/#9, ops #10/#11. Grouped contract cleanups: (a) createSpawn (E3 §4) vs startSpawn (E1 §3) — two verbs for node-side bring-up, only startSpawn in the E0 §6 contract; define both (provision vs wake) or unify. (b) refreshStorageToken (E3) missing from the E0 §6 node->CP verb list. (c) POST /spawns ownership: E0 §5a says CP 'initializes repo + writes spawn.yml' but E3 §4 says the NODE scaffolds/writes/pushes after placement — define sync vs async + who writes spawn.yml. (d) startSpawn payload carries sessionTokenPubkey but E4 §7 distributes the CP signing pubkey at enrollment — drop the redundant field. (e) Session token not channel-bound (bind to handshake transcript + add jti/replay cache + node verifies its own nodeId). (f) Enrollment token theft window — device-code style confirm + show node fingerprint to owner. (g) Separate node mTLS auth key from the E2E key-agreement static key; define rotation/revocation. Affects E0 §5a/§6, E1 §3, E3 §4, E4 §6/§7.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: cold-start-every-wake = bad UX for coaches; no latency budget
description: Found by 3/4 (arch #7, product F9, ops #5). Realistic first-token on resume = 5-15s warm-image/small-repo, 30s-2min+ cold-image/large-repo/busy-GPU (image pull + pod create + git clone /data + agent boot + skill load + key agreement + model first token). Coaches' whole value is continuity, but every 'checking in' pays full cold-start + full-thread reload — and the loyal daily user with a big thread gets the WORST latency + re-billed replay tokens. No doc owns this number. FIX: pull warm-pool/keep-alive + node repo-cache (resume=fetch not clone) + pre-pulled images + incremental/summarized thread load into MVP for coaching apps; loading-state UX in E6; set a first-token-on-resume acceptance metric. Affects E1 §3/§4, E6 §3, E7.
notes: RESOLVED (user): accept cold-start startup times for MVP; warm-pool/repo-cache/incremental-load DECLINED. Key correction: cold start = NEW session, NO conversation-thread replay. Continuity = agent reads structured files in /data at session start (not transcript-into-context). Kills the 'loyal user worst latency' + 're-bill replay' concerns. Tune idle timeout to match LLM provider prompt-cache TTL (~5min) since cache expires regardless. DOC FIX NEEDED: E1 §3 / E7 §4 / E6 §3 currently say resume 'reloads the prior thread to restore context' -- correct to 'fresh session; continuity via file reads in /data'.
close_reason: Resolved: accept cold-start times; continuity = file reads not thread replay (demo-mvp-scope §4)

title: Trust, safety & audit
description: Permission/consent enforcement; egress allowlist enforcement; audit pipeline (sidecar conversation-content logging for Spawnery-operated cloud only); abuse signals; flagging/takedown for the open registry. Design doc section 9.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-spawnery-e8-trust-safety-audit-design.md. Decisions: audit = full content on Spawnery-operated infra, encrypted, ~30d TTL auto-purge, break-glass access, disclosed (self-host exempt, BYO-on-cloud audited); review = scanner-first (LLM-as-judge over manifest+persona+skills) human-on-flag; enforcement = tiered (live block severe-only + async delist/suspend leaving user data untouched + appeal); inline classifier shares infra with review scanner (real ML build item); free-tier content-blind abuse signals. E8 is the MVP safety net standing in for deferred permission/egress enforcement.

title: Scheduler: cloud-capacity limit/queue for spawn placement
description: From the auth brainstorm (2026-06-11, A-D4): registration stays open-always (kill-switch only); capacity protection belongs at the cloud-spawn layer. Today the scheduler returns ResourceExhausted when no eligible node; enhance with a per-class capacity limit + a fair wait-queue for cloud placement (self-hosted is the owner's own capacity, unlimited). Files: internal/cp/scheduler, internal/cp/registry.

title: Remove legacy unbound enrollment token path (IssueEnrollmentToken/RunEnroll)
description: sp-2ckv.5 kept the legacy unbound enrollment token path for back-compat. Once all callers use the fingerprint-bound path (RunEnrollWithKey/IssueBoundEnrollmentToken), remove IssueEnrollmentToken/RunEnroll + their tests.

title: adoptOrStop: adopted PhaseStarting rows never swept by unreachable arm
description: Found during sp-537t.2 review: a container row still PhaseStarting (CP crashed mid-provision) that gets adopted keeps phase starting (Adopt only rebinds node_id); the unreachable arm filters on PhaseActive, so a later pod death never marks the spawn unreachable. Pre-existing store-contract behavior, not introduced by sp-537t. Also cosmetic: adoptOrStop's LiveContainer read error is silently dropped (internal/cp/server.go:210) while Adopt/MarkReachable errors are logged. Assess whether Adopt should bump phase or arm 3 should include PhaseStarting rows with a grace.

title: Reconciler hardening: distinguish transient Get errors from NotFound; bound per-tick cost
description: Follow-ups from sp-bp9w.7 quality review (both Minor, self-healing): (1) internal/cp/reconcile.go: a transient (non-NotFound) Spawns().Get error currently deletes the give-up entry, resetting the clock — a persistently flaky DB could prevent give-up. Only delete the giveUp entry on store.ErrNotFound; on other errors return without touching s.giveUp. (2) Per-tick cost: N unreachable spawns x up to 3s serial pushModel can make a tick far exceed the 5s interval and delay ctx-cancel. Consider a per-tick budget or bounded parallelism before scaling up. Non-blocking; file for later.

title: Verify multi-session Lane B live (real-model 2nd session + opencode-tui standalone boot)
description: epic sp-npxq e2e Lane B: web/e2e/multi-session.spec.ts has two test.skip(!SPAWNERY_LIVE_AGENTS) tests that need real model creds/sidecar (absent in CI/sandbox): (1) a 2nd session of a DIFFERENT real runnable answers via a model; (2) opencode-tui standalone-primary boots a usable backend (the reconciliation made opencode-tui role-agnostic: bare 'opencode' standalone if no served backend — UNVERIFIED whether bare opencode self-serves without an explicit flag). Run with SPAWNERY_LIVE_AGENTS=1 against a real env to confirm. Minor UX follow-ups also noted in review: TerminalView freshly-activated tab can render blank until resize (refit-on-activation timing); shared data-testid=status between banner and per-tab ConnStatus (cosmetic).

title: Facilitate comms between spawns (messaging / drive child spawns)
description: Let an orchestrator spawn message and/or drive sibling/child spawns it controls (built on the CP router + transparent ACP relay). Realizes the spawn<->spawn comms primitive from sp-0tw via the spawnctl surface. Same capability-gating as spawn-creation. Post-MVP per sp-0tw scope.

title: Create new spawns from inside a spawn (app-spawns-app), capability-gated
description: Allow an in-spawn caller to create child/sibling spawns via the CP (reuse runCP create path). GATED: requires an explicit manifest permission for spawn-creation, enforced against trust tier + consent; quotas/concurrency caps (sp-ach) apply. Open third-party apps get no unscoped spawn-creation (sp-0tw security crux). Post-MVP per sp-0tw scope.

title: Licensing: web/THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md (fonts + color schemes attribution)
description: Aggregate attribution: THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md crediting font families + their licenses (OFL 1.1 / Hack license) and the color schemes (mbadolato/iTerm2-Color-Schemes + per-scheme authors where listed). Verify each vendored font dir has its license file and binaries are unmodified upstream. Files: web/THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md. Depends theme corpus + fonts. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-08-terminal-appearance-settings-design.md
close_reason: THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md: 6 fonts (5 OFL + Hack MIT/Bitstream-Vera) w/ license paths, 30 color schemes + mbadolato attribution. commit a1dfd49

title: [web] Prod SPA history fallback when Go serves web/dist
description: Follow-up from sp-jpn review. Deep links (/spawn/<id>, /templates/<appId>) rely on SPA history; in dev Vite already does index.html fallback, but there is currently NO Go static-serving of web/dist. WHEN web/dist is eventually served by the Go binary, add a catch-all route that rewrites unknown non-asset paths to index.html, else refresh/deep-link will 404. Tie this to whatever task introduces serving web/dist from Go. Until then, deep-link/refresh works in dev only.

title: acpdial shim + nori terminal attach (spawnctl attach to acp-mode spawn -> shared session)
description: Tiny 'acpdial' Go shim (inverse of acpexec): pipe stdin/stdout <-> a TCP dial to 127.0.0.1:7000. Configure nori's custom agent (LocalDistribution command in ~/.nori config, or -c override) to launch acpdial so nori joins acpmux's shared goose session WITHOUT a nori fork. Wire spawnctl attach for acp-mode spawns: internal/spawnlet/terminal.go terminalInnerCmd acp branch -> launch nori (driving acpdial); add Manager argv helper mirroring TmuxAttachArgv; dispatcher 'nori' case if needed. Requires Spawn.Mode reaching spawnctl (check it is in the attach path). v1 = shared view + serialized prompts, NO arbitration / NO read-only roles (one user across devices). e2e: TWO ACP clients (node-pump web frame path + an acpdial/2nd client) see the SAME goose turn; spawnctl attach to an acp spawn launches nori joining the LIVE conversation. Depends on acpmux. See [[acp-shared-attach-architecture]].
close_reason: spawnctl attach to acp-mode spawn launches nori joining the SHARED goose session. acpdial stdio<->TCP shim (nori's custom 'spawnery' agent -> acpmux:7000); nori config baked ([[agents]] name+slug + [agents.distribution.local].command=/usr/local/bin/acpdial). terminalInnerCmd acp branch -> nori argv (spawnctl unchanged). Found+fixed load-bearing bug: nori uses STRING JSON-RPC ids; acp.Message.ID *int silently dropped nori's initialize -> introduced RawID (verbatim string|int round-trip; int helpers for self-assigned ids) across acp/pump/acpmux/ocadapter/stubagent. Reviewed APPROVED (RawID contained to agent-facing ACP wire; web Frame protocol untouched). Verified live: TestCPGooseAcpNoriAttach PASS — nori rendered real 'banana' via nori->acpdial->acpmux->goose AND a 2nd client late-joined+replayed nori's turn (shared session). Full e2e regression green.

title: acpmux: in-container single-session ACP multiplexer (relocate the fanout pump)
description: Standalone Go binary 'acpmux' that becomes the ONE ACP client of 'goose acp' (spawns it over stdio, owns a single shared session) and presents ACP-agent semantics on local TCP :7000 (where acpexec->goose listens today), multiplexing N ACP clients onto that one session: eager single goose session; each connecting client's initialize/session-new is mapped/joined to the shared session with frame-log REPLAY for late joiners; session/update notifications fan out to all clients; session/prompt from any client routes to the one session (serialized); permission requests broadcast, first response wins. Port the logic from internal/node/pump.go. Replace the dispatcher goose-acp case (acpexec goose acp -> acpmux goose acp); acpmux accepts MULTIPLE connections (acpexec was one-at-a-time). MINIMAL node change: the node pump dials :7000 as before and sees acpmux transparently as the agent (it is the first client; session created; web ChatView keeps working). Verify the existing TestCPGooseAcpEndToEnd still passes through acpmux. RISK: node reconnect/late-join must rejoin the existing session + replay (not spawn a 2nd goose) — handle in acpmux. See [[acp-shared-attach-architecture]].
close_reason: acpmux: in-container single-session ACP multiplexer delivered. Spawns 'goose acp' (one shared upstream session S), presents canonical ACP on :7000 multiplexing N downstream clients (replaces acpexec for goose-acp); replay-log+cursor for late joiners, notification fanout, serialized prompts (exact per-client result correlation), permission broadcast first-wins. Ported from internal/node/pump.go; node pump unchanged + transparent. Review found+fixed 1 blocking ordering race (turn-end result racing session/update chunks) via single-writer ordered log (TestFanout 800/800), + 4 minors (prompt-error forwarding, Stop closes conns, id-0 sentinel, perm-ordering documented cosmetic). Verified live: TestCPGooseAcpEndToEnd (transparent through acpmux) + TestAcpmuxTwoClientsShareSession (fanout+late-join replay, real goose) PASS.

title: P3: misc future follow-ups (more runnables, node direct-connect, native opencode web)
description: Deferred non-ACP-attach items split out of sp-9xr.12 when it was rescoped into the ACP shared-attach sub-epic: more runnables (codex, aider); node direct-connection capability advertised at Register; native opencode web (serve opencode's own web protocol/UI directly instead of normalizing via ocadapter; web currently stays ACP-via-ocadapter for served mode). Multi-user collaboration (concurrent drivers, turn/permission arbitration, read-only viewers) is explicitly NOT planned (use case is one user across devices, not concurrent users) — see [[acp-shared-attach-architecture]]. ghostty-web/WASM renderer dropped per owner (xterm.js stays).

title: goose-acp wiring: bridge goose stdio-ACP to the node TCP ACP listener
description: goose-acp runs 'goose acp' (ACP over stdio); the node attaches ACP over TCP (ACP_LISTEN), so goose-acp needs a stdio<->TCP bridge in the dispatcher (e.g. socat TCP-LISTEN:<port> EXEC:'goose acp', + goose OpenAI-sidecar model config). Then goose-acp -> node ACP Pump fanout -> {web ChatView, toad}. goose-tui (tmux) already works without this. Deferred from .13b.
close_reason: goose-acp wired via new generic acpexec stdio-ACP<->TCP bridge (listens on ACP_LISTEN, runs 'goose acp' piped to the node's Pump connection, fresh child per connection, deadlock-safe via StdinPipe/StdoutPipe). goose pointed at OpenAI sidecar (same env as goose-tui). Verified live: TestCPGooseAcpEndToEnd reaches ACTIVE (ACP handshake) + real model output ('banana') via Pump fanout. Reviewed: 1 blocking deadlock found+fixed (cmd.Wait blocking on net.Conn copy goroutines when child exits first) + regression test added.

title: Anthropic<->OpenAI converter so Claude Code works via OpenRouter sidecar
description: Claude Code speaks the Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages, anthropic-version header, tool_use/system blocks, Anthropic SSE). OpenRouter (sidecar upstream) is OpenAI-ONLY (/api/v1/chat/completions) — confirmed via openrouter.ai/docs. So claude-tui needs an Anthropic<->OpenAI translation proxy (a new sidecar endpoint or component handling request+streaming-response translation), then ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL->sidecar + ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_MODEL_OPTION=<openrouter model>. Binary already embedded (.13a). Mostly for testing/curiosity per owner; deferred. Consider LiteLLM's Anthropic passthrough vs a bespoke converter.
close_reason: Anthropic<->OpenAI converter in sidecar (/v1/messages: request + non-stream + streaming SSE incl tool_use), claude-tui dispatcher case wired (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL->sidecar), verified live via TestCPClaudeTuiEndToEnd (real Claude Code tool round-trip). Reviewed APPROVED.

title: lossless tmux resume via resume-argv (E3-gated)
description: Lossless conversation continuity on resume: populate agentcaps Runnable.Resume per tmux runnable + thread a 'resume' signal through StartSpawn so the node relaunches the agent reloading its session from persisted storage. BLOCKED on E3 (persistent data backends) — until then ResumeSpawn is non-lossless (fresh container) for ALL modes; sp-9xr.10 verified the non-lossless tmux path works. Revisit when E3 lands.

title: (SUB-EPIC) ACP shared multi-client attach: relocate fanout pump into the container (acpmux) + nori terminal attach
description: Same user jumping between devices sees ONE shared goose-acp conversation from BOTH the web ChatView and a terminal nori TUI (spawnctl attach to an acp-mode spawn). Relocate the ACP fanout pump OUT of the node INTO the container as a standalone 'acpmux' proxy (ACP client of goose upstream; ACP server to N local TCP clients downstream, with frame-log replay + notification fanout + permission broadcast). The node becomes a thin relay/client of acpmux. nori joins WITHOUT a fork via a tiny 'acpdial' stdio<->TCP shim configured as nori's custom agent (inverse of acpexec from sp-9xr.16). v1 scope = shared view + serialized prompts; NO arbitration / NO read-only-viewer roles (unnecessary for one-user-across-devices). Full design: [[acp-shared-attach-architecture]]. Children plan+build piece by piece.
notes:
ACP-TUI client decision (2026-06-07): use nori-cli (tilework-tech/nori-cli) — a Rust/Ratatui ACP client. spawnctl attach for acp-mode spawns would launch nori against the spawn's ACP stream, closing the acp spawnctl gap. NOTE nori has NO served/server mode (it's a client, not an agent) — so no 'nori-served' runnable; served stays opencode-only.
ACP-TUI client decision UPDATE (2026-06-07): use TOAD (https://github.com/batrachianai/toad), now embedded in the agent image (.13a), as the ACP-TUI client for spawnctl-attach to acp-mode spawns (goose-acp -> ACP fanout -> {web, toad}). Supersedes the earlier nori-cli choice for this role. Depends on goose-acp wiring.
ACP-TUI client FINAL (2026-06-07): NORI (Rust, https://github.com/tilework-tech/nori-cli), NOT toad — owner prefers Rust. nori embedded in the agent image (prebuilt linux-x86_64 binary at /usr/local/bin/nori); toad + the Python/uv toolchain removed. Remaining: wire nori for spawnctl-attach to acp-mode spawns (goose-acp -> ACP fanout -> {web, nori}).
RESCOPED 2026-06-07: was 'P3 future follow-ups' grab-bag; now the ACP shared-attach sub-epic. Non-ACP residual items (more runnables, node direct-connect, opencode web, ghostty) split to sp-9xr.17. nori is an ACP DRIVER not a stream-attacher — hence acpmux (in-container fanout) + acpdial shim instead of forking nori.
close_reason: SUB-EPIC complete: ACP shared multi-client attach delivered. .12.1 acpmux (in-container single-session ACP multiplexer, relocated fanout) + .12.2 acpdial+nori (terminal attach joins the shared session). One user across devices now sees ONE goose-acp conversation from web ChatView + terminal nori. v1 = shared view + serialized prompts (no arbitration, by design). Residual non-ACP follow-ups in sp-9xr.17.

title: Pump cleanup: trim Conn to receive-only line-reader; prune dead acp/types.ts; rename readyProbeTimeout
description: Minor non-blocking cleanups from the Plan-2 final review: (1) web/src/acp/conn.ts Conn.send (the {jsonrpc:2.0} ACP wrapper) is dead — the thin client sends raw via ReconnectingSocket.send; trim Conn to a receive-only ndjson line splitter. (2) Prune dead acp/types.ts (Message/RpcError/HistoryItem/SessionUpdate/SpawnTurn) — ACP/history vestiges. (3) internal/node: rename readyProbeTimeout -> readyTimeout + fix its doc (the standalone sp-39u probe is folded into pump.start's initialize). (4) Generic perm_request title — plumb goose's real tool/title. (5) attacher-level lifecycle tests (agent-death, startSpawn error paths).
notes: Also (from sp-j5b final review): Config.InPodAdapter in internal/node/attach.go is now fully vestigial — set in cmd/spawnlet/main.go when CONTAINER_RUNTIME==runsc but read nowhere in the node pkg (CRI lane is selected by the backend's ACP_ADAPTER env + AttachACP transport). Delete the field + its assignment. Also web/e2e/spawn-lifecycle.spec.ts:116 comment says 'node replays spawn/history on reconnect' — stale wording (pump replays via reset+log frames now); one-line comment fix.
close_reason: Pump cleanup shipped: Conn trimmed to receive-only ndjson reader; orphaned web/src/acp/types.ts deleted; readyProbeTimeout->readyTimeout (+doc); vestigial Config.InPodAdapter removed; goose's real toolCall.title now surfaced on perm_request frames (broadcast+re-send, tested); cpStream seam + startSpawn create-failure lifecycle test added. Remaining attacher success-path/exitFn/attach-fail tests moved to sp-sj8. Merged to master.

title: CRI in-pod adapter: multi-client fan-out + resumable history (match node pump)
description: The long-lived per-spawn pump + multi-client/resumable-cursor model (Docker lane, see the spawn-pump spec) is NOT applied to the CRI in-pod acpadapter, which keeps its own single-client in-pod history/replay. Bring the adapter to the same model: own the goose session, append-only frame log, fan out to N clients with resumable cursors, transient permission broadcast. Follow-up to the node-pump redesign; out of scope of that spec.
close_reason: Shipped: in-pod acpadapter is now a transparent byte-bridge with a bounded 1MiB gap buffer (oldest-whole-line eviction); node pump owns brokering/history/multi-client fan-out on the CRI lane, matching the Docker lane. internal/transcript deleted. Merged to master.

title: Hermetic test for node probeReady attach-retry + deadline budget
description: probeReady (internal/node/ready.go) retries mgr.Attach (~250ms) until a deadline, then hands the REMAINING time to awaitInitialize. The retry loop + deadline-budget arithmetic (incl. attach eating most of the 30s, leaving a tiny/near-zero timeout) is currently untested — awaitInitialize is well covered, but probeReady's loop is exercised only by the e2e happy path (Docker attach succeeds first try) and the host CRI lane (adapter-startup retry). Add a hermetic test by introducing a seam: extract the loop into a helper taking an attach closure (func() (*runtime.AttachedStream, error)), then table-test fail-N-times-then-succeed + deadline-exhausted. Raised in sp-39u code review (Minor #3, non-blocking).

title: ACP-compliant reconnect: filter redundant initialize/session-new at the node relay
description: Our model is non-standard for ACP: one long-lived agent process (goose) with MANY client sessions attaching over its lifetime, so the web client re-sends 'initialize' (+ 'session/new') on every reconnect (switch-away/back, page reload). ACP agents are normally one-process-per-connection with a single initialize. To be spec-compliant + robust across agents, the node relay should own a single ACP session per spawn: do initialize+session/new ONCE, and filter/short-circuit the client's redundant initialize/session-new on reconnect (replaying the cached responses + rewriting sessionId), forwarding only session/prompt. Bonus: this gives real agent-memory across reconnect (same session) instead of today's visual-only node replay. Spun out of the spawn-readiness discussion (sp-wi3 follow-on); relates to the node-readiness-probe work which also relies on goose tolerating re-initialize.
notes: turn-state/queueing (sp-95v) shifted the disconnect-mid-turn stuck-state surface: the broker (internal/transcript.Recorder) now stays busy=true with an inflight id that no response will ever match if the client disconnects mid-turn. The sp-r7t fix (node owns the session, filters redundant initialize/session-new on reconnect) should also reset broker turn-state on re-initialize OR have the node emit an explicit spawn/turn{state:idle} on session re-open.
close_reason: Superseded by the long-lived per-spawn pump (sp-bjd, merged). The pump owns the single goose ACP session for the spawn's whole life and issues 'initialize'+'session/new' exactly once; thin clients only send prompt frames and never re-handshake. The redundant-initialize/session-new-on-reconnect problem this bead targeted no longer exists — there is no per-connection relay re-doing the ACP handshake to filter. (CRI lane brought to the same model in sp-j5b.)

title: Make ResumeSpawn async like CreateSpawn (currently blocks the RPC during Provision)
description: sp-wi3 made CreateSpawn async (return + provision in background). ResumeSpawn (internal/cp/lifecycle.go) still runs scheduler.Provision inline holding the per-spawn lock for the node's startup latency (seconds), blocking the client RPC. For consistency + the same 'starting' UX on resume, make ResumeSpawn async too (suspended->starting->background provision->active/error). Flagged in sp-wi3 Task A review.

title: UI: WS connection status indicator (text + colored status light) in chat header
description: Replace the chat header status element (web/src/shell/AppShell.tsx: ready) with a WebSocket connection-status indicator: a status light (colored dot) + text reflecting the live ws state. States: GRAY 'connecting' while the ws is connecting; GREEN 'connected' once open/ready; YELLOW once connecting has exceeded a timeout (~5s, configurable) without connecting; RED 'error' when the ws errored. Source the state from App.tsx's ws lifecycle (openSession onopen/onerror/onclose + a 5s timer); thread a structured status (state enum + label) down to the header instead of the current free-text 'status' string. Keep data-testid="status" (and add data-status for the light/e2e). Update the e2e/unit selectors that assert status text 'ready' (chat.spec waits toHaveText('ready'); the marketplace spec too) to the new 'connected' text or a data-status check. Hermetic vitest for the indicator states + a timer test; e2e still green.
close_reason: WS status indicator shipped: useConnStatus hook (state machine + 5s slow timer) + ConnStatus component (gray-pulse connecting… / amber-pulse slow / green connected / red error, hidden when no live socket); wired into App/AppShell replacing the free-text status. Vitest (hook+component, incl. fake-timer slow + error-preserved-on-close) + tsc clean; e2e 10/10 (selectors -> 'connected'). 2 tasks two-stage reviewed. Merged to master.

title: MarkBootUnreachable doesn't sweep 'suspending' — CP crash mid-suspend strands the spawn
description: In SuspendSpawn (internal/cp/lifecycle.go), if the CP crashes after SetSuspending but before SetSuspended, the spawn is stranded in 'suspending': the in-handler SetError compensation is bypassed, and store.MarkBootUnreachable only sweeps {Starting, Active}, so boot reconcile won't recover it. Narrow window (a couple synchronous store calls; container already torn down). Fix: add 'suspending' to MarkBootUnreachable's status set (or a dedicated boot sweep that errors/recreates stranded suspending spawns). Found in sp-zer final whole-branch review. Epic sp-k20.
close_reason: Closed

title: Update web e2e chat.spec.ts to the Marketplace-default / nav-spawn flow
description: sp-wsv removed the static Chat tab + mount-time auto-spawn. web/e2e/chat.spec.ts (host-gated Playwright, excluded from npm test/vitest) still clicks getByTestId('nav-chat') and assumes chat-as-default auto-spawn — now stale. Update it to the new flow: default Marketplace, spawn an app (e.g. Secret App / spawnery/secret-app) from Browse, then click nav-spawn to open its chat. Run via npm run test:e2e on a host with browsers + the stack.
close_reason: DONE merged d16fad4. Updated web/e2e: chat.spec spawns 'Secret App' (spawnery/secret-app) from the Marketplace then chats (nav-spawn to return), marketplace.spec drops the pre-spawn status=ready waits (no auto-spawn; market is default), global-setup runs the e2e node self-hosted+EGRESS_ENFORCE=false (root-free, matches sp-11p). All 4 Playwright tests PASS (real Chromium + cp+node+vite + stubagent/sidecar containers, ~11s).

title: DockerPodBackend.StartPod: tolerate empty pod IP when floor not enforced
description: StartPod now fetches ContainerIP unconditionally and errors if empty; on master ContainerIP was fetched only when egressEnforced. Regression risk (low — our bridge sidecar always has an IP): a non-enforcing self-hosted spawn (NODE_CLASS=self-hosted + EGRESS_ENFORCE=false) whose sidecar has no bridge IP would now fail StartPod where master spawned fine. Fix options: make StartPod tolerate an empty PodIP (return handle with empty IP) + have Manager fail-closed only when egressEnforced && PodIP=='' (preserves both no-regression AND floor safety). Revisit before/with the CRI slice sp-d3t (which reworks pod IP via PodSandboxStatus). Found in sp-0wd final review.
close_reason: SUBSUMED + DONE in sp-11p (merged 80d1767): DockerPodBackend.StartPod now best-effort on ContainerIP/PID; Manager.Create fail-closes only when egressEnforced()&&PodIP==''. Required for rootless Podman (no bridge IP). Tests: TestCreateEmptyIP{FailsClosedWhenEnforced,SucceedsWhenNotEnforced} + TestDockerPodBackendStartPodToleratesMissingIPAndPID.

title: internal/node: add relay test coverage
description: internal/node has ZERO test files — this is what let the sp-7wa critical gap (CP-attached openSession still using Docker Attach) ship green through per-task review; only the final whole-branch review caught it. Add a hermetic test harness for the attacher (fake CP bidi stream + FakeRuntime + injectable attach) covering startSpawn/openSession/feed/closeSession, so the CP-attached relay path is exercised. Found in sp-7wa final review.
notes: sp-xky added the cpStream seam (internal/node/attach.go) + a startSpawn create-failure lifecycle test (attach_lifecycle_test.go) with a fakeCPStream capturing status phases. REMAINING attacher coverage for this bead: (a) startSpawn SUCCESS path + exitFn agent-death self-cleanup (delete pump + active-- + mgr.Stop) — needs a PodBackend whose Attach yields scripted-goose stdio (NewManagerWithBackend + a fake PodBackend, or reuse pump_test's scriptGoose over a fake AttachedStream); (b) mgr.Attach-failure path (Stop + ERROR); (c) the handle() Open/Close/Frame routing. The fakeCPStream + cpStream seam are already in place to build on.
close_reason: Attacher lifecycle test coverage added (internal/node/attach_lifecycle_test.go): startSpawn success->ACTIVE, agent-death exitFn self-clean (ERROR+pump-drop+capacity-release+mgr.Stop; guards sp-bjd leak fix), mgr.Attach-failure->ERROR+Stop, create-failure->ERROR, and handle() Open/Frame/Close routing. Uses a scriptedPodBackend (scripted goose stdio) + fakeCPStream over the cpStream seam from sp-xky. -race clean.

title: AttachACP: non-linux build stub
description: runtime.AttachACP lives in attach_acp_linux.go (linux-only) but server.go/node/attach.go reference it unconditionally, so GOOS=darwin go build ./internal/spawnlet fails (undefined: runtime.AttachACP). Node stack is linux-only by design + no CI cross-builds, so non-blocking. Add attach_acp_other.go (//go:build !linux) returning an 'unsupported on this OS' error so cross-platform builds compile. Found in sp-7wa final review.
close_reason: Closed

title: web: version selector on app Detail to spawn a specific (unverified) version
description: sp-t5p lets an author spawn an unverified version on their self-hosted node, but the web Detail 'Spawn' button sends no version -> CreateSpawn resolves LatestReviewed. Add a version picker on Detail (versions already shown) so onSpawn can pass a chosen version; author can then spawn their unverified version from the UI. CreateSpawnRequest.version already supported (slice 3).

title: Egress floor: IPv6 + getenvBool case-insensitivity (sp-rpa follow-ups)
description: sp-rpa nits: (1) IPv6 not covered — add ip6tables drops for fd00::/8 ULA + fe80::/10 link-local (metadata is IPv4-only so lower urgency, but exfil via IPv6 is open). (2) cmd/spawnlet getenvBool only matches 1/true/TRUE; use strings.EqualFold for false/False/yes consistency (cosmetic; cloud path ignores the knob). (3) Optional: comment that the nsenter applier assumes one-netns-per-spawn (no chain flush; safe today).

title: store: test the 0002 trust-tier migration backfill (reviewed=true -> tier='reviewed')

Add a test that opens the store at migration 0001 (goose UpTo), inserts an app_version with reviewed=1, migrates to 0002, and asserts tier='reviewed'. Low risk (no prod data) but closes the coverage gap. Backfill SQL itself is correct by inspection.

title: EPIC: tmux attach to a running spawn (raw terminal access)
description: EPIC (Phase 2): spawnctl 'tmux' attach into a running opencode spawn, concurrent with the web client, via opencode's shared server-authoritative session. mosh-server on the node; child execs into the spawn container (docker/CRI exec) running 'tmux new-session -A' -> 'opencode attach http://127.0.0.1:4096'. Control plane via CP (owner-only authz, node discovery, auto-resume if suspended); data plane is mosh UDP direct to the node (around CP for now; future: tunnel through CP<->node link for airgapped/NAT). Supersedes the earlier raw-PTY-channel framing: with opencode the 'terminal' is the opencode TUI sharing the same session, so no second relay channel is needed. See spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-05-opencode-swap-and-terminal-design.md.
design: Terminal runs INSIDE the container (node execs in); image ships opencode+tmux. Idle: only opencode-observable activity counts (terminal keystrokes bypass the adapter/SSE, so a silent TUI can be idle-suspended — accepted). Audit/E8: opencode TUI can run POST /session/:id/shell + edit tools = un-audited mutation path; accepted owner-only risk, disclosed. Dual permission answering: last-write-wins at the opencode server.
acceptance_criteria: GOAL: 'spawnctl tmux <spawn>' attaches a TUI to a running opencode spawn AND the tmux TUI and the web client see each other's inputs (shared opencode session). Attaching to a suspended spawn auto-resumes first. A second attach reattaches the existing tmux session. Owner-only enforced at CP.
notes:
SHARED-VISIBILITY PROVEN (2026-06-05) at the opencode/adapter layer: a 2nd client (simulating the in-tmux TUI) POSTed a prompt directly to the shared opencode session; the WEB side (node via adapter) saw it as ACP agent_message_chunk 'TUISIDE' through the adapter's SSE->ACP fan-out. Reverse direction is opencode's native broadcast to any SSE subscriber (the TUI). So the conceptual core of the goal is de-risked; Phase 2 is now PURELY the terminal transport plumbing: node mosh-server + exec-PTY into the container running 'tmux new -A -> opencode attach 127.0.0.1:4096' (sp-wsu.1), CP control+authz+auto-resume (sp-wsu.2), spawnctl tmux (sp-wsu.3). Node<->image TCP path (sp-5h3.4) already validated on real docker.
IMPLEMENTED + validated (standalone): spawnctl tmux -spawn <id> -> node /terminal -> mosh-server + exec tmux+opencode-attach on the shared session. Every automatable link validated incl. the in-container TUI rendering the web's prompts (shared visibility both directions). Only the final interactive mosh-client->PTY render needs a human TTY (inherent). CP-routed control plane is the remaining productionization (sp-wsu.2).

title: Conversation continuity across suspend/resume
description: Deferred from the spawn-lifecycle design (data-only resume for now). Persist the conversation transcript to the data repo per spawn.yml's conversation-history pointer on suspend; reload/replay on resume so a resumed spawn continues the prior chat. Gated per-app (some apps are stateless-per-session). Builds on the spawn lifecycle (sp); see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-spawn-lifecycle-design.md sec 5/10.

title: POST-MVP EPIC: Native spawn-to-spawn comms framework
description:
Enabling substrate for multi-spawn orchestration. Today a spawn drives ONE agent over ACP via the CP; there is no native primitive for an app/spawn to message or create sibling/child spawns ('app-spawns-app'). This epic adds a native inter-spawn communication + orchestration primitive: an orchestrator spawn that can create and drive child spawns, and/or spawn<->spawn messaging, built on the existing CP router/scheduler + transparent ACP relay.

WHY: prerequisite for native eval orchestration, sub-agent spawning, and agent-team orchestration. It is the 'communication engine' those tools build on.

SECURITY CRUX (hard requirement): spawn-spawning is sensitive under the open-marketplace trust model — open third-party apps must NOT get unscoped spawn-creation. The capability must be permissioned via the manifest permissions/consent/egress model and trust tiers (a scanner miss must stay contained). Quotas/concurrency caps from sp-ach apply.

SCOPE NOTE: post-MVP (after the demo's safety floor + marketplace). The demo's spawn engine (CP mediation slice, merged) is the foundation. Capture only; design later via brainstorming.

title: POST-MVP EPIC: Native eval orchestration (one spawn evals/optimizes another)
description:
Eval-first LLM-app-building tooling, implemented as multi-spawn orchestration on the native comms framework. Roles, each a spawn/agent over the same communication engine: SUBJECT (app under test, possibly weaker/cheaper model) + DRIVER/user-simulator (feeds inputs / a fixed eval set) + JUDGE/OPTIMIZER (stronger model). Loop: run subject on the eval set -> judge scores against a rubric -> optimizer proposes prompt/instruction edits -> re-eval -> keep if better; promote best app@sha and/or per-model prompt.

WHY IT FITS: (1) makes the 'model half' thesis concrete — per-app, per-model scores answer 'which AI is good enough for which app', and the optimizer can auto-tune an app's prompt per model. (2) Marketplace CAPABILITY signal complementing the safety trust-tiers (e.g. 'scores 8.2/10 with DeepSeek') — could become CI-for-prompts in the publish flow. (3) Dogfoods multi-spawn orchestration. (4) Immediate internal use: discharges 'verify seed apps run on DeepSeek' (sp-iui).

START NARROW: measurement-only harness first (run app over eval set vs a model, judge-score vs rubric, report per-model scores; reuse internal/acp like spawnctl) — NO optimizer. Add the optimize loop as phase 2 once the eval signal is trusted.

DESIGN CRUXES: (a) eval dataset/rubric is the real artifact — creator-authored; shapes the manifest. (b) LLM-as-judge is noisy/biased/gameable — needs rubric + held-out set + mitigations (reference-guided, pairwise, juries) to avoid reward-hacking the judge. (c) keep optimization surface tiny: {prompt variants} x {models} before tools/data. (d) cost & nondeterminism -> offline/power-creator feature, competes with live users on the single demo box. (e) app-or-infra: prototype as an app/external harness (needs the spawn-comms capability), graduate useful parts into publish/CI.

PRIOR ART (survey before building): DSPy (MIPROv2/Bootstrap), OPRO, APE, EvoPrompt, TextGrad for optimization; promptfoo/Braintrust/LangSmith/Inspect/OpenAI-Evals for harnesses; LLM-as-judge bias literature. A deep-research prompt was drafted (2026-05-31 session) to map SOTA + gap analysis. Novel angle = eval/optimize loop as native multi-spawn orchestration on an isolated, model-agnostic, versioned-app substrate feeding a marketplace.

DEPENDS ON: the native spawn-to-spawn comms framework epic. Capture only; design later via brainstorming.

title: Web: load Inter/JetBrains Mono fonts (declared in globals.css but never loaded)

Either add the font loading (preconnect + stylesheet or @fontsource deps) or drop the declarations. Surfaced by the final review of the web UI framework adoption branch. Minor/visual.

title: Web client deferrals (wss, permission shape, tool-chip update)
description: Minor follow-ups from the web ACP client final review (all documented/tracked, none blocking): (1) App.tsx hardcodes ws:// — use wss:// under HTTPS when production static-serving lands. (2) acp/client.ts session/request_permission response shape is TO-CONFIRM against the real ACP spec (kind-based option pick is a guess; secret-word demo doesn't trigger it) — confirm during a live permission-triggering run. (3) ui: onToolUpdate appends a new chip titled 'tool' instead of updating the original tool_call chip by toolCallId — use a Map<toolCallId,chip>. (4) WS OriginPatterns:['*'] dev-open — tighten at CP/auth (ties to sp-l5o authz review). Part of E6 (sp-95v).

title: Local-app data sync + ACP client (Obsidian/IDE) for spawns
description:
VISION: let users view/edit a spawn's data in a native local app (Obsidian for the wiki; an IDE for code) while the agent works it remotely, and chat with the agent from the same app via an ACP-client plugin. The killer combo is **llm-wiki + an Obsidian ACP-client plugin**: the vault shows the wiki growing in real time while the user chats in the same window.

TWO SYNC LEVELS:
- (start) READ-ONLY local mirror, continuously updated from the remote. Sidesteps bidirectional sync; edits flow through the agent (chat). For git-backed mounts this is nearly FREE (user clones their own data repo + a tiny daemon git-pulls on push). Trap: Obsidian IS an editor, so users will edit the mirror and silently lose changes on the next pull -> must be enforced read-only or upgraded to bidir.
- (target, RICHER) BIDIRECTIONAL real-time sync: user and agent collaborate LIVE on the same files -> the agent researches/writes the bulk while the user views/corrects, and both see each other's edits in real time. This is the powerful version. Hard part = real-time bidirectional sync + conflict resolution (CRDT/OT per-file e.g. Yjs for text, or fast file-granular last-write-wins). PRIOR ART: Obsidian's livesync community plugins (self-hosted CouchDB/CRDT) are a precedent for exactly this shape. Overlaps with E3's DEFERRED bidirectional sync (sp-u53).

TWO DEPLOYMENT MODES (from the ACP-client research):
- REMOTE: mirror/sync for display + ACP chat with TRIMMED fs capability (agent stays self-contained on container data); two channels (sync + ACP). A network/stdio bridge is required (all current ACP clients are stdio + local-assuming).
- SELF-HOST: mount the user's local folder straight into the spawn as the data mount, keep ACP fs capability INTACT, REWRITE PATHS (vault path <-> /app/data). No sync needed -> bidirectional by nature (same folder).

RELATED / STRATEGIC:
- We will build our OWN ACP client (existing ones are IDE-shaped: assume local checkout, want an IDE). Study Zed + VS Code ACP-client impls for the client-half rendering (session/update, tool-calls, permission prompts), drop the IDE scaffolding.
- This is arguably the answer to the wiki RETENTION problem (sp-54f) WITHOUT building proactivity (sp-37x): the wiki lives in a tool you already open daily. And it makes 'your data is yours' visceral.
- ACP-client research (network bridge required; fs trim for remote; local-mount+path-rewrite for self-host) was confirmed via deep research; bridge idea = spawnctl refactored into a transparent stdio<->Session pipe + minimal initialize capability handshake.

OUT OF SCOPE for now; parked as a future direction.
notes:
SYNC OPTIONS (a menu, none decided):
1) git pull/push loop — already have it; commit-granular, coarse.
2) CouchDB/PouchDB replication (Obsidian Self-hosted LiveSync shape) — OFF-THE-SHELF, near-real-time SAVE-granular w/ MVCC conflict-merge. Arch: CouchDB hub <-> user's Obsidian (reuse the existing LiveSync plugin) <-> a spawn-side /app/data<->CouchDB replicator (the only piece WE build); one DB per spawn. NOT a character-CRDT. Downside: needs a hosted CouchDB. = ONE OPTION.
3) CRDT keystroke-realtime (Yjs/Automerge) — true simultaneous editing, conflict-free auto-merge. DESIRED to explore eventually.

PREFERENCE (user): AVOID hosting an external DB just for sync. Favor PEER-TO-PEER: the user's local app and the spawn synchronize DIRECTLY. Fits us well — the existing client<->spawn channel (per-session E2E / direct / relay) could carry the sync updates, no central DB. Prior art for P2P: LiveSync has a P2P/WebRTC mode; Yjs (y-webrtc) + Automerge are natively P2P. IDEAL TARGET = CRDT replicas on both peers (local app + spawn's /app/data), synced over the client<->spawn channel, keystroke-realtime, no hosted DB. Note the data-substrate tension (git = durable/portable/clean-exit vs CRDT/live-store = realtime collab); likely git as source-of-truth + a realtime layer reconciling to it.

title: Mountpoint creation for data mounts (avoid per-app .gitkeep)
description: runc can't create the nested mount destination /app/<path> inside an already-read-only /app, so the mount dir must pre-exist in the bind source. Slice workaround: each app ships an empty mount dir (examples/secret-app/data/.gitkeep). Cleaner options: (a) node mkdirs appPath/<mt.Path> before the ro bind (host dir is writable; mutates the app source dir); (b) when /app is assembled per-spawn from App@sha (full design), assembly creates the mountpoints. Decide + implement when /app assembly lands. Found during the per-mount-backends e2e (mounting rw /app/data onto ro /app failed until data/ existed in the source).

title: spawnlet: image auto-pull in StartContainer/manager (spec §8 step 3)
description: runtime.Docker.StartContainer assumes the image exists locally; spec §8 step 3 said 'ensure agent base image present -- pull if missing'. Not implemented (images built locally per README). Add cli.ImagePull on not-found before create, or in Manager.Create. Final-review finding. Acceptable for the slice; needed once images come from a registry (CP integration).

title: spawnlet: thread SidecarPort to the agent's OPENAI_HOST (or drop the knob)
description: ManagerConfig.SidecarPort is configurable, but deploy/agent/entrypoint.sh + the Goose OPENAI_HOST hardcode 127.0.0.1:8080. If SidecarPort != 8080 the agent silently can't reach inference. Fix: either drop the SidecarPort knob (slice always uses 8080) or thread the port into the agent container's OPENAI_HOST env in Manager.Create. Final-review finding (whole-slice). Harmless today (nothing overrides default).

title: Billing, payments & rev-share
description: DEFERRED (post-MVP). Metering->billing; subscription tiers; BYOK tier; premium-models / private-apps gating; creator payouts and marketplace rev-share. Design doc sections 12,13 (sub-project 10).

title: Ephemeral web-device class: approve-this-browser-for-24h, auto-revoked (roast M8, deferred)

title: Argon2id passphrase fallback KEK for web device custody (deferred from web epic)

title: [web] Nav-section exhaustiveness + dedupe templates-surface grouping
description: Follow-up from sp-jpn review (non-blocking quality). (1) AppShell.topView, TemplatesView.navToTab, and Sidebar's NAV table all use a default/fallthrough, so adding a new Nav section won't fail at compile time — add an exhaustive switch / satisfies / assertNever guard so a new section is caught by tsc. (2) The 'templates-surface sections' grouping ({templates,app,my-apps,publish}) is duplicated across those same three spots — extract a single shared predicate/constant in web/src/nav/nav.ts and consume it in all three. Files: web/src/nav/nav.ts, web/src/shell/AppShell.tsx, web/src/shell/Sidebar.tsx, web/src/views/TemplatesView.tsx.

title: Address SA1019 deprecations excluded from golangci-lint config
description: The sp-ooo golangci-lint config excludes SA1019 (deprecation) to stay bug-focused and green. Track + migrate the real deprecations it would have flagged: (1) cmd/cp/main.go + cmd/spawnlet/main.go use h2c.NewHandler (golang.org/x/net/http2/h2c, deprecated) -> set http.Server.Protocols for unencrypted HTTP/2 instead. (2) internal/cp/store: bun.In is deprecated -> bun.List/bun.Tuple (3 sites: reconcile.go, spawns.go x2). (3) internal/runtime/docker.go: NetworkSettings.DefaultNetworkSettings.IPAddress deprecated (use NetworkSettings.Networks) + client.IsErrNotFound deprecated (use cerrdefs.IsNotFound). Low priority; re-enable -SA1019 in .golangci.yml once cleared.

title: Post-demo: durable/true spawn history (agent memory across reconnect + cross-device + survives suspend)
description: Beyond the visual adapter-replay: true agent memory on reconnect (ACP session/load if goose supports) and/or CP/store transcript persistence that survives suspend and works cross-device. Gated alongside E3 persistent storage. From epic sp-k20 spec §Post-demo.

title: Post-demo: on-demand history pagination (remove acpadapter 500-item cap)
description: Lazy/windowed transcript loading for long sessions; removes the slice-2 adapter 500-item cap + truncation marker. From epic sp-k20 spec §Post-demo.

title: web marketplace UI polish nits (sp-znq follow-ups)
description: Optional polish from sp-znq final review (all non-blocking): (1) App.tsx clientRef.current not nulled on re-spawn teardown (unreachable via UI since busy disables input; tidiness). (2) Detail.tsx renders createdAt as raw epoch string (int64 Connect-JSON) — format as a date. (3) Publish.tsx no client-side required-field validation (relies on CP InvalidArgument toast; a pre-submit guard saves a round-trip). (4) Publish mount rows keyed by array index (harmless given append/filter-only).

title: DeleteSpawn/StopSpawn ignore auth ok-bool (owner, _ :=) — return Unauthenticated for consistency
description: Minor consistency nit from the part-3a final review. DeleteSpawn + StopSpawn use 'owner, _ := auth.OwnerFromContext' (ignore the ok bool) and delegate ownership to stop()'s store check; ListSpawns/CreateSpawn check ok and return CodeUnauthenticated. Safe in prod (the auth.Interceptor rejects unauthenticated before the handler), but if stop() is ever called from a non-HTTP path the empty owner yields a misleading PermissionDenied. Low priority: align DeleteSpawn+StopSpawn to check ok -> Unauthenticated. Also (minor) add an explicit store-layer TestMarkDeletedFromStarting.
close_reason: Closed
